1→import Stripe from 'stripe'
     2→import type { Env, PressyBookManifest, PurchaseRecord, EmailSender } from './types.js'
     3→import { signCookie, setCookieHeader, cookieName, COOKIE_MAX_AGE, signMagicToken, verifyMagicToken, MAGIC_LINK_TTL } from './auth.js'
     4→import { buildMagicLinkEmail } from './email.js'
     5→
     6→const NO_CACHE = { 'Cache-Control': 'no-store' }
     7→const EMAIL_RE = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/
     8→const BOOK_SLUG_RE = /^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/
     9→
    10→async function sha256hex(input: string): Promise<string> {
    11→  const data = new TextEncoder().encode(input)
    12→  const hash = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', data)
    13→  return Array.from(new Uint8Array(hash)).map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')).join('')
    14→}
    15→
    16→function getStripe(secretKey: string): Stripe {
    17→  return new Stripe(secretKey, { apiVersion: '2025-02-24.acacia' })
    18→}
    19→
    20→function getBaseUrl(request: Request): string {
    21→  const url = new URL(request.url)
    22→  return `${url.protocol}//${url.host}`
    23→}
    24→
    25→function nowEpoch(): number {
    26→  return Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)
    27→}
    28→
    29→// ── Checkout ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30→
    31→export async function handleCheckout(
    32→  request: Request,
    33→  env: Env,
    34→  book: PressyBookManifest,
    35→): Promise<Response> {
    36→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
    37→  const baseUrl = getBaseUrl(request)
    38→
    39→  if (!book.paywall?.stripePriceId) {
    40→    return new Response('Book has no Stripe product configured', { status: 400 })
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({
    44→    mode: 'payment',
    45→    line_items: [{ price: book.paywall.stripePriceId, quantity: 1 }],
    46→    success_url: `${baseUrl}/api/auth/callback?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`,
    47→    cancel_url: `${baseUrl}/books/${book.slug}/`,
    48→    metadata: { bookSlug: book.slug },
    49→  })
    50→
    51→  return Response.redirect(session.url!, 303)
    52→}
    53→
    54→// ── Webhook ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55→
    56→export async function handleWebhook(
    57→  request: Request,
    58→  env: Env,
    59→): Promise<Response> {
    60→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
    61→  const body = await request.text()
    62→  const signature = request.headers.get('stripe-signature')
    63→
    64→  if (!signature) {
    65→    return new Response('Missing stripe-signature header', { status: 400 })
    66→  }
    67→
    68→  let event: Stripe.Event
    69→  try {
    70→    event = await stripe.webhooks.constructEventAsync(body, signature, env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
    71→  } catch {
    72→    return new Response('Webhook signature verification failed', { status: 400 })
    73→  }
    74→
    75→  if (event.type === 'checkout.session.completed') {
    76→    const session = event.data.object as Stripe.Checkout.Session
    77→    const bookSlug = session.metadata?.bookSlug
    78→    const email = session.customer_details?.email
    79→
    80→    if (bookSlug && email) {
    81→      const record: PurchaseRecord = {
    82→        sessionId: session.id,
    83→        email,
    84→        bookSlug,
    85→        amount: session.amount_total || 0,
    86→        purchasedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    87→      }
    88→
    89→      await env.PRESSY_KV.put(
    90→        `purchase:${email}:${bookSlug}`,
    91→        JSON.stringify(record),
    92→        { expirationTtl: 5 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 }, // 5 years
    93→      )
    94→    }
    95→  }
    96→
    97→  return new Response('ok', { status: 200 })
    98→}
    99→
   100→// ── Auth callback (post-checkout redirect) ───────────────────
   101→
   102→export async function handleAuthCallback(
   103→  request: Request,
   104→  env: Env,
   105→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   106→): Promise<Response> {
   107→  const url = new URL(request.url)
   108→  const sessionId = url.searchParams.get('session_id')
   109→
   110→  if (!sessionId) {
   111→    return new Response('Missing session_id', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  // Single-use: reject if this session_id has already been consumed
   115→  const consumedKey = `callback-consumed:${sessionId}`
   116→  const alreadyConsumed = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(consumedKey)
   117→  if (alreadyConsumed) {
   118→    return new Response('This link has already been used', { status: 410, headers: NO_CACHE })
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
   122→  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.retrieve(sessionId)
   123→
   124→  if (session.payment_status !== 'paid') {
   125→    return new Response('Payment not completed', { status: 402, headers: NO_CACHE })
   126→  }
   127→
   128→  const bookSlug = session.metadata?.bookSlug
   129→  const email = session.customer_details?.email
   130→  if (!bookSlug || !email) {
   131→    return new Response('Invalid session metadata', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   132→  }
   133→
   134→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   135→  if (!book) {
   136→    return new Response('Book not found', { status: 404, headers: NO_CACHE })
   137→  }
   138→
   139→  // Mark session as consumed (keep for 24h — long enough to prevent replay)
   140→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(consumedKey, '1', { expirationTtl: 24 * 60 * 60 })
   141→
   142→  // Record purchase in KV (in case webhook hasn't fired yet)
   143→  const record: PurchaseRecord = {
   144→    sessionId: session.id,
   145→    email,
   146→    bookSlug,
   147→    amount: session.amount_total || 0,
   148→    purchasedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   149→  }
   150→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(
   151→    `purchase:${email}:${bookSlug}`,
   152→    JSON.stringify(record),
   153→    { expirationTtl: 5 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 },
   154→  )
   155→
   156→  // Sign and set auth cookie with expiration
   157→  const cookieValue = await signCookie(
   158→    {
   159→      book: bookSlug,
   160→      email,
   161→      purchasedAt: record.purchasedAt,
   162→      sessionId,
   163→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
   164→    },
   165→    env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   166→  )
   167→
   168→  // Redirect to the book home page so the user can click "Continue Reading"
   169→  const redirectPath = `/books/${bookSlug}/`
   170→
   171→  return new Response(null, {
   172→    status: 302,
   173→    headers: {
   174→      Location: redirectPath,
   175→      'Set-Cookie': setCookieHeader(cookieName(bookSlug), cookieValue),
   176→      ...NO_CACHE,
   177→    },
   178→  })
   179→}
   180→
   181→// ── Auth status check ────────────────────────────────────────
   182→
   183→export async function handleAuthStatus(
   184→  _request: Request,
   185→  _env: Env,
   186→  authorized: boolean,
   187→): Promise<Response> {
   188→  return new Response(JSON.stringify({ authorized }), {
   189→    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   190→  })
   191→}
   192→
   193→// ── Recover purchase (sends magic link) ──────────────────────
   194→
   195→export async function handleRecoverPurchase(
   196→  request: Request,
   197→  env: Env,
   198→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   199→  sendEmail?: EmailSender,
   200→): Promise<Response> {
   201→  // Must be POST — state-changing operation
   202→  if (request.method !== 'POST') {
   203→    return new Response('Method not allowed', { status: 405, headers: NO_CACHE })
   204→  }
   205→
   206→  let body: { email?: string; book?: string }
   207→  try {
   208→    body = await request.json()
   209→  } catch {
   210→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid JSON body' }), {
   211→      status: 400,
   212→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   213→    })
   214→  }
   215→
   216→  const email = body.email?.trim().toLowerCase()
   217→  const bookSlug = body.book
   218→
   219→  if (!email || !bookSlug) {
   220→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Missing email or book' }), {
   221→      status: 400,
   222→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   223→    })
   224→  }
   225→
   226→  if (!EMAIL_RE.test(email)) {
   227→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid email format' }), {
   228→      status: 400,
   229→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   230→    })
   231→  }
   232→
   233→  if (!BOOK_SLUG_RE.test(bookSlug)) {
   234→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid book slug' }), {
   235→      status: 400,
   236→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   237→    })
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   241→  if (!book) {
   242→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Book not found' }), {
   243→      status: 400,
   244→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   245→    })
   246→  }
   247→
   248→  // Look up purchase — but DON'T reveal whether the email exists.
   249→  // Always return the same success message to prevent email enumeration.
   250→  const record = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(`purchase:${email}:${bookSlug}`)
   251→
   252→  if (record) {
   253→    // Purchase exists — send magic link email
   254→    const baseUrl = getBaseUrl(request)
   255→    const token = await signMagicToken(
   256→      { email, bookSlug, exp: nowEpoch() + MAGIC_LINK_TTL },
   257→      env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   258→    )
   259→    const verifyUrl = `${baseUrl}/api/auth/verify?token=${encodeURIComponent(token)}`
   260→
   261→    const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   262→    const bookTitle = book?.paywall?.price
   263→      ? bookSlug // We don't have the book title in the manifest, use slug
   264→      : undefined
   265→
   266→    if (!sendEmail) {
   267→      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Email sending is not configured' }), {
   268→        status: 500,
   269→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   270→      })
   271→    }
   272→
   273→    try {
   274→      await sendEmail({
   275→        to: email,
   276→        subject: 'Recover your book purchase',
   277→        html: buildMagicLinkEmail(verifyUrl, bookTitle),
   278→        verifyUrl,
   279→        bookTitle,
   280→      })
   281→    } catch {
   282→      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Failed to send email. Please try again.' }), {
   283→        status: 500,
   284→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   285→      })
   286→    }
   287→  }
   288→
   289→  // Same response regardless of whether purchase was found (anti-enumeration)
   290→  return new Response(
   291→    JSON.stringify({ sent: true, message: 'If a purchase exists for this email, a recovery link has been sent.' }),
   292→    { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE } },
   293→  )
   294→}
   295→
   296→// ── Verify magic link token ──────────────────────────────────
   297→
   298→export async function handleVerifyToken(
   299→  request: Request,
   300→  env: Env,
   301→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   302→): Promise<Response> {
   303→  const url = new URL(request.url)
   304→  const token = url.searchParams.get('token')
   305→
   306→  if (!token) {
   307→    return new Response('Missing token', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   308→  }
   309→
   310→  // Verify HMAC signature and expiration
   311→  const payload = await verifyMagicToken(token, env.COOKIE_SECRET)
   312→  if (!payload) {
   313→    return new Response('Invalid or expired link. Please request a new one.', {
   314→      status: 403,
   315→      headers: NO_CACHE,
   316→    })
   317→  }
   318→
   319→  // Validate payload fields
   320→  if (!EMAIL_RE.test(payload.email) || !BOOK_SLUG_RE.test(payload.bookSlug)) {
   321→    return new Response('Invalid token payload', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   322→  }
   323→
   324→  if (!manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === payload.bookSlug)) {
   325→    return new Response('Book not found', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   326→  }
   327→
   328→  // Single-use: prevent replay (hash full token to avoid prefix collisions)
   329→  const tokenHash = await sha256hex(token)
   330→  const consumedKey = `magic-consumed:${tokenHash}`
   331→  const alreadyConsumed = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(consumedKey)
   332→  if (alreadyConsumed) {
   333→    return new Response('This link has already been used.', { status: 410, headers: NO_CACHE })
   334→  }
   335→
   336→  // Confirm the purchase still exists in KV
   337→  const record = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(`purchase:${payload.email}:${payload.bookSlug}`)
   338→  if (!record) {
   339→    return new Response('No purchase found', { status: 404, headers: NO_CACHE })
   340→  }
   341→
   342→  const purchase: PurchaseRecord = JSON.parse(record)
   343→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === payload.bookSlug)
   344→
   345→  // Mark token as consumed (keep for the token's TTL — 15 min is plenty)
   346→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(consumedKey, '1', { expirationTtl: MAGIC_LINK_TTL })
   347→
   348→  // Issue auth cookie
   349→  const cookieValue = await signCookie(
   350→    {
   351→      book: payload.bookSlug,
   352→      email: payload.email,
   353→      purchasedAt: purchase.purchasedAt,
   354→      sessionId: purchase.sessionId,
   355→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
   356→    },
   357→    env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   358→  )
   359→
   360→  // Redirect to the book home page so the user can click "Continue Reading"
   361→  const redirectPath = `/books/${payload.bookSlug}/`
   362→
   363→  return new Response(null, {
   364→    status: 302,
   365→    headers: {
   366→      Location: redirectPath,
   367→      'Set-Cookie': setCookieHeader(cookieName(payload.bookSlug), cookieValue),
   368→      ...NO_CACHE,
   369→    },
   370→  })
   371→}
   372→

1→export interface Env {
     2→  ASSETS: Fetcher
     3→  PRESSY_KV: KVNamespace
     4→  STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: string
     5→  STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: string
     6→  COOKIE_SECRET: string
     7→}
     8→
     9→export interface SendEmailParams {
    10→  to: string
    11→  subject: string
    12→  html: string
    13→  verifyUrl: string
    14→  bookTitle?: string
    15→}
    16→
    17→export type EmailSender = (params: SendEmailParams) => Promise<void>
    18→
    19→export interface PressyMiddlewareOptions {
    20→  sendEmail?: EmailSender
    21→}
    22→
    23→export interface PressyManifest {
    24→  books: PressyBookManifest[]
    25→}
    26→
    27→export interface PressyBookManifest {
    28→  slug: string
    29→  paywall?: {
    30→    enabled: boolean
    31→    previewChapters: number
    32→    price?: string
    33→    stripePriceId?: string
    34→  }
    35→  chapters: Array<{
    36→    slug: string
    37→    order: number
    38→  }>
    39→}
    40→
    41→export interface PurchaseRecord {
    42→  sessionId: string
    43→  email: string
    44→  bookSlug: string
    45→  amount: number
    46→  purchasedAt: string
    47→}
    48→
    49→export interface CookiePayload {
    50→  book: string
    51→  email: string
    52→  purchasedAt: string
    53→  sessionId: string
    54→  /** Expiration time as Unix epoch seconds. */
    55→  exp: number
    56→}
    57→
    58→export interface MagicLinkPayload {
    59→  email: string
    60→  bookSlug: string
    61→  /** Expiration time as Unix epoch seconds. */
    62→  exp: number
    63→}

1→import { describe, it, expect, vi, type Mock } from 'vitest'
     2→
     3→// Mock stripe before any imports that reference it
     4→vi.mock('stripe', () => ({ default: vi.fn() }))
     5→
     6→import {
     7→  signCookie,
     8→  verifyCookie,
     9→  parseCookies,
    10→  signMagicToken,
    11→  verifyMagicToken,
    12→  cookieName,
    13→  COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
    14→  MAGIC_LINK_TTL,
    15→} from '../packages/@pressy/cloudflare/src/auth.js'
    16→import { createPressyMiddleware } from '../packages/@pressy/cloudflare/src/middleware.js'
    17→import type { Env, PressyManifest, PurchaseRecord } from '../packages/@pressy/cloudflare/src/types.js'
    18→
    19→// ── Shared fixtures ─────────────────────────────────────────
    20→
    21→const TEST_SECRET = 'test-secret-key-that-is-at-least-32-chars!'
    22→
    23→const TEST_MANIFEST: PressyManifest = {
    24→  books: [
    25→    {
    26→      slug: 'test-book',
    27→      paywall: {
    28→        enabled: true,
    29→        previewChapters: 2,
    30→        price: '$9.99',
    31→        stripePriceId: 'price_test',
    32→      },
    33→      chapters: [
    34→        { slug: 'chapter-1', order: 0 },
    35→        { slug: 'chapter-2', order: 1 },
    36→        { slug: 'chapter-3', order: 2 }, // first paid chapter
    37→        { slug: 'chapter-4', order: 3 },
    38→      ],
    39→    },
    40→  ],
    41→}
    42→
    43→function nowEpoch(): number {
    44→  return Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)
    45→}
    46→
    47→async function sha256hex(input: string): Promise<string> {
    48→  const data = new TextEncoder().encode(input)
    49→  const hash = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', data)
    50→  return Array.from(new Uint8Array(hash)).map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')).join('')
    51→}
    52→
    53→function createMockEnv(kvStore = new Map<string, string>()) {
    54→  return {
    55→    ASSETS: {
    56→      fetch: async () =>
    57→        new Response(JSON.stringify(TEST_MANIFEST), {
    58→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
    59→        }),
    60→    },
    61→    PRESSY_KV: {
    62→      get: async (key: string) => kvStore.get(key) ?? null,
    63→      put: async (key: string, value: string, _opts?: unknown) => {
    64→        kvStore.set(key, value)
    65→      },
    66→    },
    67→    STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: 'sk_test_fake',
    68→    STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: 'whsec_fake',
    69→    COOKIE_SECRET: TEST_SECRET,
    70→  } as unknown as Env
    71→}
    72→
    73→async function requestWithAuthCookie(url: string, bookSlug: string): Promise<Request> {
    74→  const payload = {
    75→    book: bookSlug,
    76→    email: 'test@example.com',
    77→    purchasedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    78→    sessionId: 'sess_test',
    79→    exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
    80→  }
    81→  const signed = await signCookie(payload, TEST_SECRET)
    82→  return new Request(url, {
    83→    headers: { cookie: `${cookieName(bookSlug)}=${signed}` },
    84→  })
    85→}
    86→
    87→// ── Auth crypto ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    88→
    89→describe('auth crypto', () => {
    90→  it('signCookie → verifyCookie roundtrip returns original payload', async () => {
    91→    const payload = {
    92→      book: 'test-book',
    93→      email: 'user@example.com',
    94→      purchasedAt: '2024-01-15T00:00:00.000Z',
    95→      sessionId: 'sess_abc',
    96→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
    97→    }
    98→    const signed = await signCookie(payload, TEST_SECRET)
    99→    const verified = await verifyCookie(signed, TEST_SECRET)
   100→    expect(verified).toEqual(payload)
   101→  })
   102→
   103→  it('expired cookie returns null', async () => {
   104→    const payload = {
   105→      book: 'test-book',
   106→      email: 'user@example.com',
   107→      purchasedAt: '2024-01-15T00:00:00.000Z',
   108→      sessionId: 'sess_abc',
   109→      exp: nowEpoch() - 60, // expired 1 minute ago
   110→    }
   111→    const signed = await signCookie(payload, TEST_SECRET)
   112→    const verified = await verifyCookie(signed, TEST_SECRET)
   113→    expect(verified).toBeNull()
   114→  })
   115→
   116→  it('tampered cookie returns null', async () => {
   117→    // Sign a legitimate cookie, then swap in a different payload
   118→    // while keeping the original HMAC — should be rejected by signature check
   119→    const original = {
   120→      book: 'test-book',
   121→      email: 'user@example.com',
   122→      purchasedAt: '2024-01-15T00:00:00.000Z',
   123→      sessionId: 'sess_abc',
   124→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
   125→    }
   126→    const signed = await signCookie(original, TEST_SECRET)
   127→    const originalSig = signed.slice(signed.indexOf('.') + 1)
   128→
   129→    // Create a different payload and encode it the same way
   130→    const forged = { ...original, email: 'attacker@evil.com' }
   131→    const forgedPayloadB64 = btoa(JSON.stringify(forged))
   132→      .replace(/\+/g, '-').replace(/\//g, '_').replace(/=+$/, '')
   133→
   134→    const tampered = `${forgedPayloadB64}.${originalSig}`
   135→    const verified = await verifyCookie(tampered, TEST_SECRET)
   136→    expect(verified).toBeNull()
   137→  })
   138→
   139→  it('signMagicToken → verifyMagicToken roundtrip works', async () => {
   140→    const payload = {
   141→      email: 'user@example.com',
   142→      bookSlug: 'test-book',
   143→      exp: nowEpoch() + MAGIC_LINK_TTL,
   144→    }
   145→    const token = await signMagicToken(payload, TEST_SECRET)
   146→    const verified = await verifyMagicToken(token, TEST_SECRET)
   147→    expect(verified).toEqual(payload)
   148→  })
   149→
   150→  it('HKDF key separation — cookie token cannot verify as magic token', async () => {
   151→    const cookiePayload = {
   152→      book: 'test-book',
   153→      email: 'user@example.com',
   154→      purchasedAt: '2024-01-15T00:00:00.000Z',
   155→      sessionId: 'sess_abc',
   156→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
   157→    }
   158→    const cookieToken = await signCookie(cookiePayload, TEST_SECRET)
   159→
   160→    // A cookie token should NOT verify as a magic token (different derived key)
   161→    const magicResult = await verifyMagicToken(cookieToken, TEST_SECRET)
   162→    expect(magicResult).toBeNull()
   163→  })
   164→
   165→  it('HKDF key separation — magic token cannot verify as cookie', async () => {
   166→    const magicPayload = {
   167→      email: 'user@example.com',
   168→      bookSlug: 'test-book',
   169→      exp: nowEpoch() + MAGIC_LINK_TTL,
   170→    }
   171→    const magicToken = await signMagicToken(magicPayload, TEST_SECRET)
   172→
   173→    // A magic token should NOT verify as a cookie (different derived key)
   174→    const cookieResult = await verifyCookie(magicToken, TEST_SECRET)
   175→    expect(cookieResult).toBeNull()
   176→  })
   177→})

Perform a security audit of the dependency and supply chain security of /Users/blittle/dev/pressy.

1. Run `pnpm audit` to check for known vulnerabilities
2. Read all package.json files in the repo
3. Read pnpm-lock.yaml (just the first ~200 lines to understand lockfile presence and format)
4. Read any CI/CD files in .github/
5. Check for:
   - Known vulnerable dependencies (via pnpm audit output)
   - Overly permissive version ranges (e.g., * or >= without upper bound)
   - Dev dependencies that shouldn't be in production
   - Pinned vs unpinned dependencies
   - Lock file integrity
   - CI/CD pipeline security (secrets exposure, untrusted input in workflows, pull_request_target usage)
   - GitHub Actions using unpinned actions (e.g., @main instead of @sha)
   - Any hardcoded secrets, API keys, or credentials in the codebase
   - .env files or secret patterns committed to git
   - .gitignore completeness

Report ALL findings with file paths, line numbers, severity, and specifics. Do NOT suggest fixes.

1→/// <reference lib="webworker" />
     2→import { precacheAndRoute, cleanupOutdatedCaches } from 'workbox-precaching'
     3→import { registerRoute, NavigationRoute, Route } from 'workbox-routing'
     4→import { NetworkFirst, CacheFirst, StaleWhileRevalidate } from 'workbox-strategies'
     5→import { ExpirationPlugin } from 'workbox-expiration'
     6→import {
     7→  isBookChapterNavigation,
     8→  needsTrailingSlashRedirect,
     9→  normalizeCacheUrl,
    10→  prepareResponseForCache,
    11→} from './sw-logic'
    12→
    13→declare const self: ServiceWorkerGlobalScope
    14→
    15→const OFFLINE_CACHE = 'pressy-offline'
    16→// Use the SW's own location to derive the base path for offline.html,
    17→// so it works on subpath deployments (e.g. /pressy/pr-preview/pr-1/sw.js)
    18→const SW_BASE = new URL('./', (self as unknown as ServiceWorkerGlobalScope).location.href).pathname
    19→const OFFLINE_URL = `${SW_BASE}offline.html`
    20→const BOOK_CACHE = 'pressy-offline-books'
    21→
    22→// Precache all static assets (injected by build tool)
    23→precacheAndRoute(self.__WB_MANIFEST, {
    24→  ignoreURLParametersMatching: [/^page$/],
    25→})
    26→
    27→// Clean up old caches
    28→cleanupOutdatedCaches()
    29→
    30→// Cache the offline fallback page on install
    31→self.addEventListener('install', (event) => {
    32→  event.waitUntil(
    33→    caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE).then((cache) => cache.add(OFFLINE_URL))
    34→  )
    35→  self.skipWaiting()
    36→})
    37→
    38→self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {
    39→  event.waitUntil(self.clients.claim())
    40→})
    41→
    42→// Plugin that respects Cache-Control: no-store (workbox ignores it by default).
    43→// This prevents paywall/auth-gated responses from polluting the SW cache.
    44→const respectNoStore = {
    45→  cacheWillUpdate: async ({ response }: { response: Response }) => {
    46→    const cc = response.headers.get('Cache-Control')
    47→    if (cc && cc.includes('no-store')) return null
    48→    return response
    49→  },
    50→}
    51→
    52→// Navigation: NetworkFirst with offline fallback
    53→const navigationHandler = new NetworkFirst({
    54→  cacheName: 'pressy-pages',
    55→  networkTimeoutSeconds: 3,
    56→  plugins: [respectNoStore],
    57→})
    58→
    59→// Offline-cached book chapters: try the book cache first,
    60→// then fall through to the normal navigation handler.
    61→// Registered BEFORE the general NavigationRoute so book chapter
    62→// URLs are checked against the offline book cache first.
    63→// ignoreSearch: true so ?page=last (backward chapter nav) matches
    64→// the cached response for the base URL.
    65→registerRoute(
    66→  new Route(
    67→    ({ request, url }) => isBookChapterNavigation(url.pathname, request.mode),
    68→    async (params) => {
    69→      // Redirect non-trailing-slash URLs so relative asset paths in the
    70→      // HTML (e.g. ../../../assets/foo.js) resolve against the correct
    71→      // directory. Without the slash, the browser uses the parent path.
    72→      if (needsTrailingSlashRedirect(params.url.pathname)) {
    73→        const dest = new URL(params.url.href)
    74→        dest.pathname += '/'
    75→        return Response.redirect(dest.href, 302)
    76→      }
    77→
    78→      // Check offline book cache (trailing-slash keys)
    79→      const bookCache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
    80→      const cached = await bookCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
    81→      if (cached) return cached
    82→
    83→      // Fall through to network
    84→      try {
    85→        return await navigationHandler.handle(params)
    86→      } catch {
    87→        // Network failed — try the general navigation cache with ignoreSearch
    88→        // so ?page=last variants still hit cached chapter pages
    89→        const pagesCache = await caches.open('pressy-pages')
    90→        const pagesCached = await pagesCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
    91→        if (pagesCached) return pagesCached
    92→
    93→        const cache = await caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE)
    94→        const fallback = await cache.match(OFFLINE_URL)
    95→        return fallback || Response.error()
    96→      }
    97→    }
    98→  )
    99→)
   100→
   101→// General navigation: NetworkFirst with offline fallback
   102→registerRoute(
   103→  new NavigationRoute(async (params) => {
   104→    try {
   105→      return await navigationHandler.handle(params)
   106→    } catch {
   107→      // Network failed — try offline book cache and pressy-pages with
   108→      // ignoreSearch so ?page=last (backward chapter nav) still resolves
   109→      const bookCache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   110→      const bookCached = await bookCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
   111→      if (bookCached) return bookCached
   112→
   113→      const pagesCache = await caches.open('pressy-pages')
   114→      const pagesCached = await pagesCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
   115→      if (pagesCached) return pagesCached
   116→
   117→      // Last resort — serve offline fallback
   118→      const cache = await caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE)
   119→      const fallback = await cache.match(OFFLINE_URL)
   120→      return fallback || Response.error()
   121→    }
   122→  })
   123→)
   124→
   125→// Cache images with CacheFirst strategy (exclude PWA icons — they're
   126→// updated on each deploy and should not be stuck behind a stale cache)
   127→registerRoute(
   128→  ({ request, url }) =>
   129→    request.destination === 'image' &&
   130→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/icon-192.png') &&
   131→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/icon-512.png') &&
   132→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/favicon.png'),
   133→  new CacheFirst({
   134→    cacheName: 'pressy-images',
   135→    plugins: [
   136→      new ExpirationPlugin({
   137→        maxEntries: 100,
   138→        maxAgeSeconds: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30, // 30 days
   139→      }),
   140→    ],
   141→  })
   142→)
   143→
   144→// Cache fonts with CacheFirst strategy
   145→registerRoute(
   146→  ({ request }) => request.destination === 'font',
   147→  new CacheFirst({
   148→    cacheName: 'pressy-fonts',
   149→    plugins: [
   150→      new ExpirationPlugin({
   151→        maxEntries: 20,
   152→        maxAgeSeconds: 60 * 60 * 24 * 365, // 1 year
   153→      }),
   154→    ],
   155→  })
   156→)
   157→
   158→// Cache CSS and JS with NetworkFirst so deploys are picked up immediately.
   159→// Vite hashes filenames so old URLs are never re-requested — the main
   160→// benefit of caching here is offline support, not speed.
   161→registerRoute(
   162→  ({ request }) =>
   163→    request.destination === 'style' || request.destination === 'script',
   164→  new NetworkFirst({
   165→    cacheName: 'pressy-static',
   166→    networkTimeoutSeconds: 3,
   167→    plugins: [respectNoStore],
   168→  })
   169→)
   170→
   171→// Message handler for manual cache operations
   172→self.addEventListener('message', async (event) => {
   173→  if (event.data?.type === 'CACHE_BOOK') {
   174→    const { bookSlug, urls } = event.data
   175→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   176→    let completed = 0
   177→
   178→    for (const url of urls) {
   179→      try {
   180→        const response = await fetch(url)
   181→        if (response.ok) {
   182→          // Strip the redirected flag — navigation requests reject
   183→          // already-followed redirect responses from the cache.
   184→          const clean = prepareResponseForCache(response)
   185→          // Normalize cache key to trailing-slash so it matches
   186→          // redirected navigation requests (e.g. /chapter-2/)
   187→          const cacheUrl = normalizeCacheUrl(url)
   188→          await cache.put(cacheUrl, clean)
   189→        }
   190→      } catch (err) {
   191→        console.error(`Failed to cache ${url}:`, err)
   192→      }
   193→
   194→      completed++
   195→      // Report progress back to client
   196→      event.source?.postMessage({
   197→        type: 'CACHE_PROGRESS',
   198→        bookSlug,
   199→        current: completed,
   200→        total: urls.length,
   201→      })
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    // Notify client that caching is complete
   205→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_COMPLETE', bookSlug, urls })
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  if (event.data?.type === 'GET_CACHE_STATUS') {
   209→    const { urls } = event.data
   210→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   211→    const cached: string[] = []
   212→
   213→    for (const url of urls) {
   214→      // Normalize to trailing-slash to match CACHE_BOOK keys
   215→      const matchUrl = normalizeCacheUrl(url)
   216→      const response = await cache.match(matchUrl)
   217→      if (response) {
   218→        cached.push(url)
   219→      }
   220→    }
   221→
   222→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_STATUS', cached })
   223→  }
   224→
   225→  if (event.data?.type === 'CLEAR_BOOK_CACHE') {
   226→    const { bookSlug } = event.data
   227→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   228→    const keys = await cache.keys()
   229→
   230→    for (const request of keys) {
   231→      if (request.url.includes(`/books/${bookSlug}`)) {
   232→        await cache.delete(request)
   233→      }
   234→    }
   235→
   236→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_CLEARED', bookSlug })
   237→  }
   238→
   239→  if (event.data?.type === 'INVALIDATE_BOOK_PAGES') {
   240→    const { bookSlug } = event.data
   241→    const cacheNames = ['pressy-pages', 'pressy-static']
   242→    for (const name of cacheNames) {
   243→      const cache = await caches.open(name)
   244→      const keys = await cache.keys()
   245→      for (const request of keys) {
   246→        if (request.url.includes(`/books/${bookSlug}`)) {
   247→          await cache.delete(request)
   248→        }
   249→      }
   250→    }
   251→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'BOOK_PAGES_INVALIDATED', bookSlug })
   252→  }
   253→
   254→  if (event.data?.type === 'SKIP_WAITING') {
   255→    self.skipWaiting()
   256→  }
   257→})
   258→

input.prompt:
Perform a security audit specifically on the paywall enforcement and content protection in /Users/blittle/dev/pressy.

1. Read the Cloudflare middleware/worker code in packages/@pressy/cloudflare/src/
2. Read the paywall component in packages/@pressy/components/src/Paywall.tsx
3. Read the Vite plugin that handles content/routes in packages/pressy/src/vite/
4. Read the build output logic - how are chapters built and served
5. Read the moby-dick example's pressy.config.ts and any worker/deployment config
6. Read the e2e paywall test

Analyze the full paywall flow:
- Can paid content be accessed without payment by:
  - Directly requesting the chapter HTML file?
  - Manipulating the service worker cache?
  - Bypassing the Cloudflare worker entirely (direct asset access)?
  - Forging or replaying auth cookies?
  - Timing attacks on HMAC verification?
  - Manipulating the client-side unlock state in IndexedDB?
  - Accessing the build output directly?
- Is paid content included in the initial HTML/JS bundle?
- Does the static site generator embed full chapter content that could be extracted?
- Are there any race conditions in the checkout callback flow?
- Can the paywall inline page leak content through its HTML?

Report ALL findings with file paths, line numbers, severity, and specific code snippets. Do NOT suggest fixes.

Plugin[] { 26→ let root: string 27→ let contentDir: string 28→ let manifest: ContentManifest = { books: [], articles: [] } 29→ let routes: Route[] = [] 30→ 31→ const pwaEnabled = config.pwa?.enabled 36→ shortName: config.pwa?.shortName || config.site.title, 37→ } 38→ // Resolve favicon: explicit favicon > icon192 > icon > null (use generated placeholder) 39→ const faviconPath = config.pwa?.favicon || config.pwa?.icon192 || config.pwa?.icon 40→ const hasCustomFavicon = !!faviconPath 41→ 42→ // Auto-discover user custom CSS at theme/custom.css 43→ let customCssPath: string | null = null 44→ 45→ const contentDiscovery = { 46→ async discoverContent(): Promise { 47→ const books = await this.discoverBooks() 48→ const articles = await this.discoverArticles() 49→ return { books, articles } 50→ }, 51→ 52→ async discoverBooks(): Promise { 53→ const booksDir = join(contentDir, 'books') 54→ if (!existsSync(booksDir)) return [] 55→ 56→ const bookDirs = await fastGlob('*', { 57→ cwd: booksDir, 58→ onlyDirectories: true, 59→ }) 60→ 61→ const books: Book[] = [] 62→ 63→ for (const bookSlug of bookDirs) { 64→ const bookPath = join(booksDir, bookSlug) 65→ const metadataPath = join(bookPath, '_book.yaml') 66→ 67→ if (!existsSync(metadataPath)) continue 68→ 69→ const metadataContent = readFileSync(metadataPath, 'utf-8') 70→ const metadata = yaml.parse(metadataContent) 71→ 72→ // Find chapters 73→ const chapterFiles = await fastGlob('*.mdx', { 74→ cwd: bookPath, 75→ ignore: ['_*.mdx'], 76→ }) 77→ 78→ const chapters: Chapter[] = chapterFiles 79→ .map((file): Chapter | 86→ const content = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8') 87→ const { data } = matter(content) 88→ 89→ return { 90→ slug, 91→ title: data.title || slug.replace(/-/g, ' '), 92→ order, 93→ filePath, 94→ wordCount: content.split(/\s+/).length, 95→ readingTime: Math.ceil(content.split(/\s+/).length / 200), 96→ } 97→ }) 98→ .filter((c): c is Chapter => c !== null) 99→ .sort((a, b) => a.order - b.order)     27→// IndexedDB for reading progress
    28→const DB_NAME = 'pressy'
    29→const DB_VERSION = 2
    30→const PROGRESS_STORE = 'reading-progress'
    31→const UNLOCKS_STORE = 'unlocks'
    32→const BOOKMARKS_STORE = 'bookmarks'
    33→
    34→let db: IDBDatabase | null = null
    35→
    36→async function initDB(): Promise<IDBDatabase> {
    37→  if (db) return db
    38→
    39→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    40→    const request = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION)
    41→

    43→    request.onblocked = () => reject(new Error('Database upgrade blocked by open connection'))
    44→    request.onsuccess = () => {
    45→      db = request.result
    46→      resolve(db)
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    request.onupgradeneeded = (event) => {
    50→      const database = (event.target as IDBOpenDBRequest).result
    51→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(PROGRESS_STORE)) {
    52→        database.createObjectStore(PROGRESS_STORE, { keyPath: 'chapterSlug' })
    53→      }
    54→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(UNLOCKS_STORE)) {
    55→        database.createObjectStore(UNLOCKS_STORE, { keyPath: 'bookSlug' })
    56→      }
    57→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(BOOKMARKS_STORE)) {
    58→        database.createObjectStore(BOOKMARKS_STORE, { keyPath: 'id' })
    59→      }
    60→    }
    61→  })
    62→}
    63→
    64→export async function saveReadingProgress(progress: ReadingProgress): Promise<void> {
    65→  const database = await initDB()
    66→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    67→    const tx = database.transaction(PROGRESS_STORE, 'readwrite')
    68→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)
    69→    const request = store.put(progress)
    70→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve()
    71→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    72→  })
    73→}
    74→
    75→export async function getReadingProgress(chapterSlug: string): Promise<ReadingProgress | null> {
    76→  const database = await initDB()
    77→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    78→    const tx = database.transaction(PROGRESS_STORE, 'readonly')
    79→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)
    80→    const request = store.get(chapterSlug)
    81→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve(request.result || null)
    82→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    83→  })
    84→}
    85→
    86→export async function getAllReadingProgress(): Promise<ReadingProgress[]> {
    87→  const database = await initDB()
    88→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    89→    const tx = database.transaction(PROGRESS_STORE, 'readonly')
    90→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)
    91→    const request = store.getAll()
    92→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve(request.result || [])
    93→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    94→  })
    95→}

1→/**
     2→ * Pure functions extracted from sw.ts for testability.
     3→ * These handle URL routing, normalization, and response preparation
     4→ * for the service worker's offline caching logic.
     5→ */
     6→
     7→/**
     8→ * Returns true if the given pathname matches a book chapter URL.
     9→ * Does NOT anchor to the start of the path, so it works on subpath
    10→ * deployments (e.g. /pressy/pr-preview/pr-1/books/flatland/chapter-2/).
    11→ */
    12→export function isBookChapterNavigation(
    13→  pathname: string,
    14→  requestMode: string,
    15→): boolean {
    16→  return requestMode === 'navigate' && /\/books\/[^/]+\/[^/]+/.test(pathname)
    17→}
    18→
    19→/**
    20→ * Returns true if a book chapter pathname needs a trailing-slash redirect.
    21→ * HTML uses relative asset paths that resolve based on the URL's "directory" —
    22→ * without a trailing slash the browser resolves one level too high.
    23→ */
    24→export function needsTrailingSlashRedirect(pathname: string): boolean {
    25→  return !pathname.endsWith('/')
    26→}
    27→
    28→/**
    29→ * Normalize a URL string to have a trailing slash so cache keys are
    30→ * consistent with the redirected navigation requests.
    31→ */
    32→export function normalizeCacheUrl(url: string): string {
    33→  // For full URLs, parse and normalize the pathname
    34→  if (url.startsWith('http://') || url.startsWith('https://')) {
    35→    const parsed = new URL(url)
    36→    if (!parsed.pathname.endsWith('/')) {
    37→      parsed.pathname += '/'
    38→    }
    39→    return parsed.href
    40→  }
    41→  return url.endsWith('/') ? url : url + '/'
    42→}
    43→
    44→/**
    45→ * Strip the `redirected` flag from a response so it can be served from
    46→ * the cache for navigation requests. Browsers reject cache hits that
    47→ * have the redirected flag set on navigation requests.
    48→ */
    49→export function prepareResponseForCache(response: Response): Response {
    50→  if (!response.redirected) return response
    51→  return new Response(response.body, {
    52→    status: response.status,
    53→    statusText: response.statusText,
    54→    headers: response.headers,
    55→  })
    56→}
    57→

1→/// <reference lib="webworker" />
     2→import { precacheAndRoute, cleanupOutdatedCaches } from 'workbox-precaching'
     3→import { registerRoute, NavigationRoute, Route } from 'workbox-routing'
     4→import { NetworkFirst, CacheFirst, StaleWhileRevalidate } from 'workbox-strategies'
     5→import { ExpirationPlugin } from 'workbox-expiration'
     6→import {
     7→  isBookChapterNavigation,
     8→  needsTrailingSlashRedirect,
     9→  normalizeCacheUrl,
    10→  prepareResponseForCache,
    11→} from './sw-logic'
    12→
    13→declare const self: ServiceWorkerGlobalScope
    14→
    15→const OFFLINE_CACHE = 'pressy-offline'
    16→// Use the SW's own location to derive the base path for offline.html,
    17→// so it works on subpath deployments (e.g. /pressy/pr-preview/pr-1/sw.js)
    18→const SW_BASE = new URL('./', (self as unknown as ServiceWorkerGlobalScope).location.href).pathname
    19→const OFFLINE_URL = `${SW_BASE}offline.html`
    20→const BOOK_CACHE = 'pressy-offline-books'
    21→
    22→// Precache all static assets (injected by build tool)
    23→precacheAndRoute(self.__WB_MANIFEST, {
    24→  ignoreURLParametersMatching: [/^page$/],
    25→})
    26→
    27→// Clean up old caches
    28→cleanupOutdatedCaches()
    29→
    30→// Cache the offline fallback page on install
    31→self.addEventListener('install', (event) => {
    32→  event.waitUntil(
    33→    caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE).then((cache) => cache.add(OFFLINE_URL))
    34→  )
    35→  self.skipWaiting()
    36→})
    37→
    38→self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {
    39→  event.waitUntil(self.clients.claim())
    40→})
    41→
    42→// Plugin that respects Cache-Control: no-store (workbox ignores it by default).
    43→// This prevents paywall/auth-gated responses from polluting the SW cache.
    44→const respectNoStore = {
    45→  cacheWillUpdate: async ({ response }: { response: Response }) => {
    46→    const cc = response.headers.get('Cache-Control')
    47→    if (cc && cc.includes('no-store')) return null
    48→    return response
    49→  },
    50→}
    51→
    52→// Navigation: NetworkFirst with offline fallback
    53→const navigationHandler = new NetworkFirst({
    54→  cacheName: 'pressy-pages',
    55→  networkTimeoutSeconds: 3,
    56→  plugins: [respectNoStore],
    57→})
    58→
    59→// Offline-cached book chapters: try the book cache first,
    60→// then fall through to the normal navigation handler.
    61→// Registered BEFORE the general NavigationRoute so book chapter
    62→// URLs are checked against the offline book cache first.
    63→// ignoreSearch: true so ?page=last (backward chapter nav) matches
    64→// the cached response for the base URL.
    65→registerRoute(
    66→  new Route(
    67→    ({ request, url }) => isBookChapterNavigation(url.pathname, request.mode),
    68→    async (params) => {
    69→      // Redirect non-trailing-slash URLs so relative asset paths in the
    70→      // HTML (e.g. ../../../assets/foo.js) resolve against the correct
    71→      // directory. Without the slash, the browser uses the parent path.
    72→      if (needsTrailingSlashRedirect(params.url.pathname)) {
    73→        const dest = new URL(params.url.href)
    74→        dest.pathname += '/'
    75→        return Response.redirect(dest.href, 302)
    76→      }
    77→
    78→      // Check offline book cache (trailing-slash keys)
    79→      const bookCache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
    80→      const cached = await bookCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
    81→      if (cached) return cached
    82→
    83→      // Fall through to network
    84→      try {
    85→        return await navigationHandler.handle(params)
    86→      } catch {
    87→        // Network failed — try the general navigation cache with ignoreSearch
    88→        // so ?page=last variants still hit cached chapter pages
    89→        const pagesCache = await caches.open('pressy-pages')
    90→        const pagesCached = await pagesCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
    91→        if (pagesCached) return pagesCached
    92→
    93→        const cache = await caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE)
    94→        const fallback = await cache.match(OFFLINE_URL)
    95→        return fallback || Response.error()
    96→      }
    97→    }
    98→  )
    99→)
   100→
   101→// General navigation: NetworkFirst with offline fallback
   102→registerRoute(
   103→  new NavigationRoute(async (params) => {
   104→    try {
   105→      return await navigationHandler.handle(params)
   106→    } catch {
   107→      // Network failed — try offline book cache and pressy-pages with
   108→      // ignoreSearch so ?page=last (backward chapter nav) still resolves
   109→      const bookCache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   110→      const bookCached = await bookCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
   111→      if (bookCached) return bookCached
   112→
   113→      const pagesCache = await caches.open('pressy-pages')
   114→      const pagesCached = await pagesCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
   115→      if (pagesCached) return pagesCached
   116→
   117→      // Last resort — serve offline fallback
   118→      const cache = await caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE)
   119→      const fallback = await cache.match(OFFLINE_URL)
   120→      return fallback || Response.error()
   121→    }
   122→  })
   123→)
   124→
   125→// Cache images with CacheFirst strategy (exclude PWA icons — they're
   126→// updated on each deploy and should not be stuck behind a stale cache)
   127→registerRoute(
   128→  ({ request, url }) =>
   129→    request.destination === 'image' &&
   130→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/icon-192.png') &&
   131→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/icon-512.png') &&
   132→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/favicon.png'),
   133→  new CacheFirst({
   134→    cacheName: 'pressy-images',
   135→    plugins: [
   136→      new ExpirationPlugin({
   137→        maxEntries: 100,
   138→        maxAgeSeconds: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30, // 30 days
   139→      }),
   140→    ],
   141→  })
   142→)
   143→
   144→// Cache fonts with CacheFirst strategy
   145→registerRoute(
   146→  ({ request }) => request.destination === 'font',
   147→  new CacheFirst({
   148→    cacheName: 'pressy-fonts',
   149→    plugins: [
   150→      new ExpirationPlugin({
   151→        maxEntries: 20,
   152→        maxAgeSeconds: 60 * 60 * 24 * 365, // 1 year
   153→      }),
   154→    ],
   155→  })
   156→)
   157→
   158→// Cache CSS and JS with NetworkFirst so deploys are picked up immediately.
   159→// Vite hashes filenames so old URLs are never re-requested — the main
   160→// benefit of caching here is offline support, not speed.
   161→registerRoute(
   162→  ({ request }) =>
   163→    request.destination === 'style' || request.destination === 'script',
   164→  new NetworkFirst({
   165→    cacheName: 'pressy-static',
   166→    networkTimeoutSeconds: 3,
   167→    plugins: [respectNoStore],
   168→  })
   169→)
   170→
   171→// Message handler for manual cache operations
   172→self.addEventListener('message', async (event) => {
   173→  if (event.data?.type === 'CACHE_BOOK') {
   174→    const { bookSlug, urls } = event.data
   175→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   176→    let completed = 0
   177→
   178→    for (const url of urls) {
   179→      try {
   180→        const response = await fetch(url)
   181→        if (response.ok) {
   182→          // Strip the redirected flag — navigation requests reject
   183→          // already-followed redirect responses from the cache.
   184→          const clean = prepareResponseForCache(response)
   185→          // Normalize cache key to trailing-slash so it matches
   186→          // redirected navigation requests (e.g. /chapter-2/)
   187→          const cacheUrl = normalizeCacheUrl(url)
   188→          await cache.put(cacheUrl, clean)
   189→        }
   190→      } catch (err) {
   191→        console.error(`Failed to cache ${url}:`, err)
   192→      }
   193→
   194→      completed++
   195→      // Report progress back to client
   196→      event.source?.postMessage({
   197→        type: 'CACHE_PROGRESS',
   198→        bookSlug,
   199→        current: completed,
   200→        total: urls.length,
   201→      })
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    // Notify client that caching is complete
   205→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_COMPLETE', bookSlug, urls })
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  if (event.data?.type === 'GET_CACHE_STATUS') {
   209→    const { urls } = event.data
   210→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   211→    const cached: string[] = []
   212→
   213→    for (const url of urls) {
   214→      // Normalize to trailing-slash to match CACHE_BOOK keys
   215→      const matchUrl = normalizeCacheUrl(url)
   216→      const response = await cache.match(matchUrl)
   217→      if (response) {
   218→        cached.push(url)
   219→      }
   220→    }
   221→
   222→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_STATUS', cached })
   223→  }
   224→
   225→  if (event.data?.type === 'CLEAR_BOOK_CACHE') {
   226→    const { bookSlug } = event.data
   227→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   228→    const keys = await cache.keys()
   229→
   230→    for (const request of keys) {
   231→      if (request.url.includes(`/books/${bookSlug}`)) {
   232→        await cache.delete(request)
   233→      }
   234→    }
   235→
   236→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_CLEARED', bookSlug })
   237→  }
   238→
   239→  if (event.data?.type === 'INVALIDATE_BOOK_PAGES') {
   240→    const { bookSlug } = event.data
   241→    const cacheNames = ['pressy-pages', 'pressy-static']
   242→    for (const name of cacheNames) {
   243→      const cache = await caches.open(name)
   244→      const keys = await cache.keys()
   245→      for (const request of keys) {
   246→        if (request.url.includes(`/books/${bookSlug}`)) {
   247→          await cache.delete(request)
   248→        }
   249→      }
   250→    }
   251→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'BOOK_PAGES_INVALIDATED', bookSlug })
   252→  }
   253→
   254→  if (event.data?.type === 'SKIP_WAITING') {
   255→    self.skipWaiting()
   256→  }
   257→})
   258→

1→name: Deploy Moby-Dick to Cloudflare Pages
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  push:
     5→    branches: [main]
     6→  workflow_dispatch:
     7→
     8→concurrency:
     9→  group: deploy-moby-dick
    10→  cancel-in-progress: true
    11→
    12→jobs:
    13→  deploy:
    14→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15→    steps:
    16→      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    17→
    18→      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
    19→
    20→      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    21→        with:
    22→          node-version: 20
    23→          cache: pnpm
    24→
    25→      - name: Install dependencies

    27→// IndexedDB for reading progress
    28→const DB_NAME = 'pressy'
    29→const DB_VERSION = 2
    30→const PROGRESS_STORE = 'reading-progress'
    31→const UNLOCKS_STORE = 'unlocks'
    32→const BOOKMARKS_STORE = 'bookmarks'
    33→
    34→let db: IDBDatabase | null = null
    35→
    36→async function initDB(): Promise<IDBDatabase> {
    37→  if (db) return db
    38→
    39→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    40→    const request = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION)
    41→
    42→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    43→    request.onblocked = () => reject(new Error('Database upgrade blocked by open connection'))
    44→    request.onsuccess = () => {
    45→      db = request.result
    46→      resolve(db)
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    request.onupgradeneeded = (event) => {
    50→      const database = (event.target as IDBOpenDBRequest).result
    51→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(PROGRESS_STORE)) {
    52→        database.createObjectStore(PROGRESS_STORE, { keyPath: 'chapterSlug' })
    53→      }
    54→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(UNLOCKS_STORE)) {
    55→        database.createObjectStore(UNLOCKS_STORE, { keyPath: 'bookSlug' })
    56→      }
    57→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(BOOKMARKS_STORE)) {
    58→        database.createObjectStore(BOOKMARKS_STORE, { keyPath: 'id' })
    59→      }
    60→    }
    61→  })
    62→}
    63→
    64→export async function saveReadingProgress(progress: ReadingProgress): Promise<void> {
    65→  const database = await initDB()
    66→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    67→    const tx = database.transaction(PROGRESS_STORE, 'readwrite')
    68→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)
    69→    const request = store.put(progress)
    70→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve()
    71→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    72→  })
    73→}
    74→
    75→export async function getReadingProgress(chapterSlug: string): Promise<ReadingProgress | null> {
    76→  const database = await initDB()
    77→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    78→    const tx = database.transaction(PROGRESS_STORE, 'readonly')
    79→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)
    80→    const request = store.get(chapterSlug)
    81→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve(request.result || null)
    82→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    83→  })
    84→}

    90→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)

     3→const ENCODER = new TextEncoder()
     4→
     5→/** Cookie lifetime: 1 year in seconds. */
     6→export const COOKIE_MAX_AGE = 365 * 24 * 60 * 60
     7→
     8→/** Magic link token lifetime: 15 minutes in seconds. */
     9→export const MAGIC_LINK_TTL = 15 * 60
    10→
    11→/** HKDF contexts for key separation. */
    12→const COOKIE_KEY_CONTEXT = 'pressy-cookie-v1'
    13→const MAGIC_KEY_CONTEXT = 'pressy-magic-v1'
    14→
    15→/**
    16→ * Derive a purpose-specific HMAC signing key from the master secret using HKDF.
    17→ * This ensures cookie keys and magic-link keys are cryptographically separated.
    18→ */
    19→async function deriveKey(secret: string, context: string): Promise<CryptoKey> {
    20→  const masterKey = await crypto.subtle.importKey(
    21→    'raw',
    22→    ENCODER.encode(secret),
    23→    'HKDF',
    24→    false,
    25→    ['deriveBits'],
    26→  )
    27→
    28→  const derivedBits = await crypto.subtle.deriveBits(
    29→    {
    30→      name: 'HKDF',
    31→      hash: 'SHA-256',
    32→      salt: new Uint8Array(32), // fixed zero salt — context label provides separation
    33→      info: ENCODER.encode(context),
    34→    },
    35→    masterKey,
    36→    256,
    37→  )
    38→
    39→  return crypto.subtle.importKey(
    40→    'raw',
    41→    derivedBits,
    42→    { name: 'HMAC', hash: 'SHA-256' },
    43→    false,
    44→    ['sign', 'verify'],
    45→  )
    46→}
    47→
    48→function base64url(buffer: ArrayBuffer): string {
    49→  const bytes = new Uint8Array(buffer)
    50→  let binary = ''
    51→  for (const b of bytes) binary += String.fromCharCode(b)
    52→  return btoa(binary).replace(/\+/g, '-').replace(/\//g, '_').replace(/=+$/, '')
    53→}
    54→
    55→function base64urlDecode(str: string): Uint8Array {
    56→  const padded = str.replace(/-/g, '+').replace(/_/g, '/') + '=='.slice(0, (4 - (str.length % 4)) % 4)
    57→  const binary = atob(padded)
    58→  const bytes = new Uint8Array(binary.length)
    59→  for (let i = 0; i < binary.length; i++) bytes[i] = binary.charCodeAt(i)
    60→  return bytes
    61→}
    62→
    63→/**
    64→ * Sign an arbitrary JSON payload with HMAC-SHA256.
    65→ * Returns `base64url(payload).base64url(hmac)`.
    66→ */
    67→async function sign<T>(payload: T, key: CryptoKey): Promise<string> {
    68→  const payloadJson = JSON.stringify(payload)
    69→  const payloadB64 = base64url(ENCODER.encode(payloadJson).buffer as ArrayBuffer)
    70→  const signature = await crypto.subtle.sign('HMAC', key, ENCODER.encode(payloadB64))
    71→  return `${payloadB64}.${base64url(signature)}`
    72→}
    73→
    74→/**
    75→ * Verify an HMAC-signed token and return the parsed payload.
    76→ * Returns null if the signature is invalid, the token is malformed,
    77→ * or the payload has an `exp` field that is in the past.
    78→ */
    79→async function verify<T extends { exp?: number }>(token: string, key: CryptoKey): Promise<T | null> {
    80→  const dotIdx = token.indexOf('.')
    81→  if (dotIdx === -1) return null
    82→
    83→  const payloadB64 = token.slice(0, dotIdx)
    84→  const sigB64 = token.slice(dotIdx + 1)
    85→
    86→  try {
    87→    const sigBytes = base64urlDecode(sigB64)
    88→    const valid = await crypto.subtle.verify(
    89→      'HMAC', key, sigBytes.buffer as ArrayBuffer, ENCODER.encode(payloadB64),
    90→    )
    91→    if (!valid) return null
    92→
    93→    const payloadBytes = base64urlDecode(payloadB64)
    94→    const payloadJson = new TextDecoder().decode(payloadBytes)
    95→    const parsed = JSON.parse(payloadJson) as T
    96→
    97→    // Reject expired tokens
    98→    if (parsed.exp !== undefined && parsed.exp < Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)) {
    99→      return null
   100→    }
   101→
   102→    return parsed
   103→  } catch {
   104→    return null
   105→  }
   106→}

   108→// ── Cookie helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────
   109→
   110→export function cookieName(bookSlug: string): string {

   113→
   114→export async function signCookie(payload: CookiePayload, secret: string): Promise<string> {
   115→  const key = await deriveKey(secret, COOKIE_KEY_CONTEXT)
   116→  return sign(payload, key)
   117→}
   118→
   119→export async function verifyCookie(cookieValue: string, secret: string): Promise<CookiePayload | null> {
   120→  const key = await deriveKey(secret, COOKIE_KEY_CONTEXT)
   121→  return verify<CookiePayload>(cookieValue, key)
   122→}
   123→
   124→export function setCookieHeader(name: string, value: string): string {
   125→  return `${name}=${value}; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Lax; Path=/; Max-Age=${COOKIE_MAX_AGE}`
   126→}
   127→
   128→export function parseCookies(header: string): Record<string, string> {
   129→  const cookies: Record<string, string> = {}
   130→  for (const pair of header.split(';')) {
   131→    const [key, ...rest] = pair.trim().split('=')
   132→    if (key) cookies[key.trim()] = rest.join('=').trim()
   133→  }
   134→  return cookies
   135→}
   136→
   137→// ── Magic link token helpers ─────────────────────────────────
   138→
   139→export async function signMagicToken(payload: MagicLinkPayload, secret: string): Promise<string> {
   140→  const key = await deriveKey(secret, MAGIC_KEY_CONTEXT)
   141→  return sign(payload, key)
   142→}
   143→
   144→export async function verifyMagicToken(token: string, secret: string): Promise<MagicLinkPayload | null> {
   145→  const key = await deriveKey(secret, MAGIC_KEY_CONTEXT)
   146→  return verify<MagicLinkPayload>(token, key)
   147→}

    31→export async function handleCheckout(
    32→  request: Request,
    33→  env: Env,
    34→  book: PressyBookManifest,
    35→): Promise<Response> {
    36→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
    37→  const baseUrl = getBaseUrl(request)
    38→
    39→  if (!book.paywall?.stripePriceId) {
    40→    return new Response('Book has no Stripe product configured', { status: 400 })
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({
    44→    mode: 'payment',
    45→    line_items: [{ price: book.paywall.stripePriceId, quantity: 1 }],
    46→    success_url: `${baseUrl}/api/auth/callback?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`,
    47→    cancel_url: `${baseUrl}/books/${book.slug}/`,
    48→    metadata: { bookSlug: book.slug },
    49→  })
    50→
    51→  return Response.redirect(session.url!, 303)

    56→export async function handleWebhook(
    57→  request: Request,
    58→  env: Env,
    59→): Promise<Response> {
    60→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
    61→  const body = await request.text()
    62→  const signature = request.headers.get('stripe-signature')
    63→
    64→  if (!signature) {
    65→    return new Response('Missing stripe-signature header', { status: 400 })

    71→  } catch {
    72→    return new Response('Webhook signature verification failed', { status: 400 })
    73→  }
    74→
    75→  if (event.type === 'checkout.session.completed') {
    76→    const session = event.data.object as Stripe.Checkout.Session
    77→    const bookSlug = session.metadata?.bookSlug
    78→    const email = session.customer_details?.email
    79→
    80→    if (bookSlug && email) {

    82→        sessionId: session.id,
    83→        email,
    84→        bookSlug,
    85→        amount: session.amount_total || 0,
    86→        purchasedAt: new Date().toISOString(),

    95→  }

   100→// ── Auth callback (post-checkout redirect) ───────────────────
   101→
   102→export async function handleAuthCallback(
   103→  request: Request,
   104→  env: Env,
   105→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   106→): Promise<Response> {
   107→  const url = new URL(request.url)
   108→  const sessionId = url.searchParams.get('session_id')
   109→
   110→  if (!sessionId) {
   111→    return new Response('Missing session_id', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  // Single-use: reject if this session_id has already been consumed
   115→  const consumedKey = `callback-consumed:${sessionId}`
   116→  const alreadyConsumed = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(consumedKey)
   117→  if (alreadyConsumed) {
   118→    return new Response('This link has already been used', { status: 410, headers: NO_CACHE })
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
   122→  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.retrieve(sessionId)
   123→
   124→  if (session.payment_status !== 'paid') {
   125→    return new Response('Payment not completed', { status: 402, headers: NO_CACHE })
   126→  }
   127→
   128→  const bookSlug = session.metadata?.bookSlug
   129→  const email = session.customer_details?.email
   130→  if (!bookSlug || !email) {
   131→    return new Response('Invalid session metadata', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   132→  }
   133→
   134→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   135→  if (!book) {
   136→    return new Response('Book not found', { status: 404, headers: NO_CACHE })
   137→  }
   138→
   139→  // Mark session as consumed (keep for 24h — long enough to prevent replay)
   140→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(consumedKey, '1', { expirationTtl: 24 * 60 * 60 })
   141→
   142→  // Record purchase in KV (in case webhook hasn't fired yet)
   143→  const record: PurchaseRecord = {
   144→    sessionId: session.id,
   145→    email,
   146→    bookSlug,
   147→    amount: session.amount_total || 0,
   148→    purchasedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   149→  }
   150→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(
   151→    `purchase:${email}:${bookSlug}`,
   152→    JSON.stringify(record),
   153→    { expirationTtl: 5 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 },
   154→  )
   155→
   156→  // Sign and set auth cookie with expiration
   157→  const cookieValue = await signCookie(
   158→    {
   159→      book: bookSlug,
   160→      email,
   161→      purchasedAt: record.purchasedAt,
   162→      sessionId,
   163→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
   164→    },
   165→    env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   166→  )
   167→
   168→  // Redirect to the book home page so the user can click "Continue Reading"
   169→  const redirectPath = `/books/${bookSlug}/`
   170→
   171→  return new Response(null, {
   172→    status: 302,
   173→    headers: {
   174→      Location: redirectPath,
   175→      'Set-Cookie': setCookieHeader(cookieName(bookSlug), cookieValue),
   176→      ...NO_CACHE,
   177→    },
   178→  })
   179→}
   180→
   181→// ── Auth status check ────────────────────────────────────────
   182→
   183→export async function handleAuthStatus(
   184→  _request: Request,
   185→  _env: Env,
   186→  authorized: boolean,
   187→): Promise<Response> {
   188→  return new Response(JSON.stringify({ authorized }), {
   189→    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   190→  })
   191→}
   192→
   193→// ── Recover purchase (sends magic link) ──────────────────────
   194→
   195→export async function handleRecoverPurchase(
   196→  request: Request,
   197→  env: Env,
   198→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   199→  sendEmail?: EmailSender,
   200→): Promise<Response> {
   201→  // Must be POST — state-changing operation
   202→  if (request.method !== 'POST') {
   203→    return new Response('Method not allowed', { status: 405, headers: NO_CACHE })
   204→  }
   205→
   206→  let body: { email?: string; book?: string }
   207→  try {
   208→    body = await request.json()
   209→  } catch {
   210→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid JSON body' }), {
   211→      status: 400,
   212→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   213→    })
   214→  }
   215→
   216→  const email = body.email?.trim().toLowerCase()
   217→  const bookSlug = body.book
   218→
   219→  if (!email || !bookSlug) {
   220→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Missing email or book' }), {
   221→      status: 400,
   222→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   223→    })
   224→  }
   225→
   226→  if (!EMAIL_RE.test(email)) {
   227→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid email format' }), {
   228→      status: 400,
   229→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   230→    })
   231→  }
   232→
   233→  if (!BOOK_SLUG_RE.test(bookSlug)) {
   234→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid book slug' }), {
   235→      status: 400,
   236→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   237→    })
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   241→  if (!book) {
   242→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Book not found' }), {
   243→      status: 400,
   244→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   245→    })
   246→  }
   247→
   248→  // Look up purchase — but DON'T reveal whether the email exists.
   249→  // Always return the same success message to prevent email enumeration.
   250→  const record = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(`purchase:${email}:${bookSlug}`)
   251→
   252→  if (record) {
   253→    // Purchase exists — send magic link email
   254→    const baseUrl = getBaseUrl(request)
   255→    const token = await signMagicToken(
   256→      { email, bookSlug, exp: nowEpoch() + MAGIC_LINK_TTL },
   257→      env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   258→    )
   259→    const verifyUrl = `${baseUrl}/api/auth/verify?token=${encodeURIComponent(token)}`
   260→
   261→    const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   262→    const bookTitle = book?.paywall?.price
   263→      ? bookSlug // We don't have the book title in the manifest, use slug
   264→      : undefined
   265→
   266→    if (!sendEmail) {
   267→      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Email sending is not configured' }), {
   268→        status: 500,
   269→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   270→      })
   271→    }
   272→
   273→    try {
   274→      await sendEmail({
   275→        to: email,
   276→        subject: 'Recover your book purchase',
   277→        html: buildMagicLinkEmail(verifyUrl, bookTitle),
   278→        verifyUrl,
   279→        bookTitle,
   280→      })
   281→    } catch {
   282→      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Failed to send email. Please try again.' }), {
   283→        status: 500,
   284→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   285→      })
   286→    }
   287→  }
   288→
   289→  // Same response regardless of whether purchase was found (anti-enumeration)
   290→  return new Response(
   291→    JSON.stringify({ sent: true, message: 'If a purchase exists for this email, a recovery link has been sent.' }),
   292→    { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE } },
   293→  )
   294→}
   295→
   296→// ── Verify magic link token ──────────────────────────────────
   297→
   298→export async function handleVerifyToken(
   299→  request: Request,
   300→  env: Env,
   301→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   302→): Promise<Response> {
   303→  const url = new URL(request.url)
   304→  const token = url.searchParams.get('token')
   305→
   306→  if (!token) {
   307→    return new Response('Missing token', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   308→  }
   309→
   310→  // Verify HMAC signature and expiration
   311→  const payload = await verifyMagicToken(token, env.COOKIE_SECRET)
   312→  if (!payload) {
   313→    return new Response('Invalid or expired link. Please request a new one.', {
   314→      status: 403,
   315→      headers: NO_CACHE,
   316→    })
   317→  }
   318→
   319→  // Validate payload fields
   320→  if (!EMAIL_RE.test(payload.email) || !BOOK_SLUG_RE.test(payload.bookSlug)) {
   321→    return new Response('Invalid token payload', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   322→  }
   323→
   324→  if (!manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === payload.bookSlug)) {
   325→    return new Response('Book not found', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   326→  }
   327→
   328→  // Single-use: prevent replay (hash full token to avoid prefix collisions)
   329→  const tokenHash = await sha256hex(token)
   330→  const consumedKey = `magic-consumed:${tokenHash}`
   331→  const alreadyConsumed = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(consumedKey)
   332→  if (alreadyConsumed) {
   333→    return new Response('This link has already been used.', { status: 410, headers: NO_CACHE })
   334→  }
   335→
   336→  // Confirm the purchase still exists in KV
   337→  const record = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(`purchase:${payload.email}:${payload.bookSlug}`)
   338→  if (!record) {
   339→    return new Response('No purchase found', { status: 404, headers: NO_CACHE })
   340→  }
   341→
   342→  const purchase: PurchaseRecord = JSON.parse(record)
   343→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === payload.bookSlug)
   344→
   345→  // Mark token as consumed (keep for the token's TTL — 15 min is plenty)
   346→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(consumedKey, '1', { expirationTtl: MAGIC_LINK_TTL })
   347→
   348→  // Issue auth cookie
   349→  const cookieValue = await signCookie(
   350→    {
   351→      book: payload.bookSlug,
   352→      email: payload.email,
   353→      purchasedAt: purchase.purchasedAt,
   354→      sessionId: purchase.sessionId,
   355→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
   356→    },
   357→    env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   358→  )
   359→
   360→  // Redirect to the book home page so the user can click "Continue Reading"
   361→  const redirectPath = `/books/${payload.bookSlug}/`
   362→
   363→  return new Response(null, {
   364→    status: 302,
   365→    headers: {
   366→      Location: redirectPath,
   367→      'Set-Cookie': setCookieHeader(cookieName(payload.bookSlug), cookieValue),
   368→      ...NO_CACHE,
   369→    },
   370→  })
   371→}
   372→

1→import { test, expect } from '@playwright/test'
     2→
     3→/**
     4→ * Intercept JS bundles and enable the paywall config.
     5→ * The flatland build has `paywall:{enabled:!1}` — we replace it
     6→ * with an enabled paywall so client-side components render.
     7→ */
     8→async function enablePaywall(page: import('@playwright/test').Page) {
     9→  await page.route('**/assets/*.js', async (route) => {
    10→    const response = await route.fetch()
    11→    let body = await response.text()
    12→    body = body.replaceAll(
    13→      'paywall:{enabled:!1}',
    14→      'paywall:{enabled:!0,previewChapters:2,price:"$9.99",stripePriceId:"price_test"}',
    15→    )
    16→    await route.fulfill({ response, body })
    17→  })
    18→}
    19→
    20→async function mockAuthStatus(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, authorized: boolean) {
    21→  await page.route('**/api/auth/status*', async (route) => {
    22→    await route.fulfill({
    23→      status: 200,
    24→      contentType: 'application/json',
    25→      body: JSON.stringify({ authorized }),
    26→    })
    27→  })
    28→}
    29→
    30→async function mockRecoverEndpoint(page: import('@playwright/test').Page) {
    31→  await page.route('**/api/auth/recover', async (route) => {
    32→    await route.fulfill({
    33→      status: 200,
    34→      contentType: 'application/json',
    35→      body: JSON.stringify({ sent: true }),
    36→    })
    37→  })
    38→}
    39→
    40→test.describe('paywall UI', () => {
    41→  test('unauthorized user sees paywall UI', async ({ page }) => {
    42→    await enablePaywall(page)
    43→    await mockAuthStatus(page, false)
    44→
    45→    await page.goto('/books/flatland/')
    46→    await page.waitForSelector('#app')
    47→
    48→    // Purchase CTA with price should be visible
    49→    await expect(page.getByText('$9.99')).toBeVisible()
    50→
    51→    // "Already purchased?" recovery link should be visible
    52→    await expect(page.getByText('Already purchased?')).toBeVisible()
    53→  })
    54→
    55→  test('authorized user sees no paywall UI', async ({ page }) => {
    56→    await enablePaywall(page)
    57→    await mockAuthStatus(page, true)
    58→
    59→    await page.goto('/books/flatland/')
    60→    await page.waitForSelector('#app')
    61→
    62→    // No purchase CTA
    63→    await expect(page.getByText('$9.99')).not.toBeVisible()
    64→
    65→    // No recovery link
    66→    await expect(page.getByText('Already purchased?')).not.toBeVisible()
    67→  })
    68→
    69→  test('recovery link flow', async ({ page }) => {
    70→    await enablePaywall(page)
    71→    await mockAuthStatus(page, false)
    72→    await mockRecoverEndpoint(page)
    73→
    74→    await page.goto('/books/flatland/')
    75→    await page.waitForSelector('#app')
    76→
    77→    // Click "Already purchased?"
    78→    await page.getByText('Already purchased?').click()
    79→
    80→    // Email form should appear
    81→    const emailInput = page.locator('input[type="email"]')
    82→    await expect(emailInput).toBeVisible()
    83→
    84→    // Fill email and submit
    85→    await emailInput.fill('reader@example.com')
    86→    await page.getByText('Send Recovery Link').click()
    87→
    88→    // Success message should appear
    89→    await expect(page.getByText('Check your email for a recovery link.')).toBeVisible()
    90→  })
    91→})

    13→declare const self: ServiceWorkerGlobalScope

    15→const OFFLINE_CACHE = 'pressy-offline'
    16→// Use the SW's own location to derive the base path for offline.html,
    17→// so it works on subpath deployments (e.g. /pressy/pr-preview/pr-1/sw.js)
    18→const SW_BASE = new URL('./', (self as unknown as ServiceWorkerGlobalScope).location.href).pathname
    19→const OFFLINE_URL = `${SW_BASE}offline.html`
    20→const BOOK_CACHE = 'pressy-offline-books'
    21→
    22→// Precache all static assets (injected by build tool)

    24→  ignoreURLParametersMatching: [/^page$/],
    25→})
    26→
    27→// Clean up old caches
    28→cleanupOutdatedCaches()
    29→
    30→// Cache the offline fallback page on install
    31→self.addEventListener('install', (event) => {
    32→  event.waitUntil(
    33→    caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE).then((cache) => cache.add(OFFLINE_URL))
    34→  )
    35→  self.skipWaiting()
    36→})
    37→
    38→self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {
    39→  event.waitUntil(self.clients.claim())
    40→})
