1→import Stripe from 'stripe'
     2→import type { Env, PressyBookManifest, PurchaseRecord, EmailSender } from './types.js'
     3→import { signCookie, setCookieHeader, cookieName, COOKIE_MAX_AGE, signMagicToken, verifyMagicToken, MAGIC_LINK_TTL } from './auth.js'
     4→import { buildMagicLinkEmail } from './email.js'
     5→
     6→const NO_CACHE = { 'Cache-Control': 'no-store' }
     7→const EMAIL_RE = /^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/
     8→const BOOK_SLUG_RE = /^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$/
     9→
    10→async function sha256hex(input: string): Promise<string> {
    11→  const data = new TextEncoder().encode(input)
    12→  const hash = await crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', data)
    13→  return Array.from(new Uint8Array(hash)).map((b) => b.toString(16).padStart(2, '0')).join('')
    14→}
    15→
    16→function getStripe(secretKey: string): Stripe {
    17→  return new Stripe(secretKey, { apiVersion: '2025-02-24.acacia' })
    18→}
    19→
    20→function getBaseUrl(request: Request): string {
    21→  const url = new URL(request.url)
    22→  return `${url.protocol}//${url.host}`
    23→}
    24→
    25→function nowEpoch(): number {
    26→  return Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000)
    27→}
    28→
    29→// ── Checkout ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    30→
    31→export async function handleCheckout(
    32→  request: Request,
    33→  env: Env,
    34→  book: PressyBookManifest,
    35→): Promise<Response> {
    36→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
    37→  const baseUrl = getBaseUrl(request)
    38→
    39→  if (!book.paywall?.stripePriceId) {
    40→    return new Response('Book has no Stripe product configured', { status: 400 })
    41→  }
    42→
    43→  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.create({
    44→    mode: 'payment',
    45→    line_items: [{ price: book.paywall.stripePriceId, quantity: 1 }],
    46→    success_url: `${baseUrl}/api/auth/callback?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`,
    47→    cancel_url: `${baseUrl}/books/${book.slug}/`,
    48→    metadata: { bookSlug: book.slug },
    49→  })
    50→
    51→  return Response.redirect(session.url!, 303)
    52→}
    53→
    54→// ── Webhook ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55→
    56→export async function handleWebhook(
    57→  request: Request,
    58→  env: Env,
    59→): Promise<Response> {
    60→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
    61→  const body = await request.text()
    62→  const signature = request.headers.get('stripe-signature')
    63→
    64→  if (!signature) {
    65→    return new Response('Missing stripe-signature header', { status: 400 })
    66→  }
    67→
    68→  let event: Stripe.Event
    69→  try {
    70→    event = await stripe.webhooks.constructEventAsync(body, signature, env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
    71→  } catch {
    72→    return new Response('Webhook signature verification failed', { status: 400 })
    73→  }
    74→
    75→  if (event.type === 'checkout.session.completed') {
    76→    const session = event.data.object as Stripe.Checkout.Session
    77→    const bookSlug = session.metadata?.bookSlug
    78→    const email = session.customer_details?.email
    79→
    80→    if (bookSlug && email) {
    81→      const record: PurchaseRecord = {
    82→        sessionId: session.id,
    83→        email,
    84→        bookSlug,
    85→        amount: session.amount_total || 0,
    86→        purchasedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
    87→      }
    88→
    89→      await env.PRESSY_KV.put(
    90→        `purchase:${email}:${bookSlug}`,
    91→        JSON.stringify(record),
    92→        { expirationTtl: 5 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 }, // 5 years
    93→      )
    94→    }
    95→  }
    96→
    97→  return new Response('ok', { status: 200 })
    98→}
    99→
   100→// ── Auth callback (post-checkout redirect) ───────────────────
   101→
   102→export async function handleAuthCallback(
   103→  request: Request,
   104→  env: Env,
   105→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   106→): Promise<Response> {
   107→  const url = new URL(request.url)
   108→  const sessionId = url.searchParams.get('session_id')
   109→
   110→  if (!sessionId) {
   111→    return new Response('Missing session_id', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  // Single-use: reject if this session_id has already been consumed
   115→  const consumedKey = `callback-consumed:${sessionId}`
   116→  const alreadyConsumed = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(consumedKey)
   117→  if (alreadyConsumed) {
   118→    return new Response('This link has already been used', { status: 410, headers: NO_CACHE })
   119→  }
   120→
   121→  const stripe = getStripe(env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY)
   122→  const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.retrieve(sessionId)
   123→
   124→  if (session.payment_status !== 'paid') {
   125→    return new Response('Payment not completed', { status: 402, headers: NO_CACHE })
   126→  }
   127→
   128→  const bookSlug = session.metadata?.bookSlug
   129→  const email = session.customer_details?.email
   130→  if (!bookSlug || !email) {
   131→    return new Response('Invalid session metadata', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   132→  }
   133→
   134→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   135→  if (!book) {
   136→    return new Response('Book not found', { status: 404, headers: NO_CACHE })
   137→  }
   138→
   139→  // Mark session as consumed (keep for 24h — long enough to prevent replay)
   140→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(consumedKey, '1', { expirationTtl: 24 * 60 * 60 })
   141→
   142→  // Record purchase in KV (in case webhook hasn't fired yet)
   143→  const record: PurchaseRecord = {
   144→    sessionId: session.id,
   145→    email,
   146→    bookSlug,
   147→    amount: session.amount_total || 0,
   148→    purchasedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   149→  }
   150→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(
   151→    `purchase:${email}:${bookSlug}`,
   152→    JSON.stringify(record),
   153→    { expirationTtl: 5 * 365 * 24 * 60 * 60 },
   154→  )
   155→
   156→  // Sign and set auth cookie with expiration
   157→  const cookieValue = await signCookie(
   158→    {
   159→      book: bookSlug,
   160→      email,
   161→      purchasedAt: record.purchasedAt,
   162→      sessionId,
   163→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
   164→    },
   165→    env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   166→  )
   167→
   168→  // Redirect to the book home page so the user can click "Continue Reading"
   169→  const redirectPath = `/books/${bookSlug}/`
   170→
   171→  return new Response(null, {
   172→    status: 302,
   173→    headers: {
   174→      Location: redirectPath,
   175→      'Set-Cookie': setCookieHeader(cookieName(bookSlug), cookieValue),
   176→      ...NO_CACHE,
   177→    },
   178→  })
   179→}
   180→
   181→// ── Auth status check ────────────────────────────────────────
   182→
   183→export async function handleAuthStatus(
   184→  _request: Request,
   185→  _env: Env,
   186→  authorized: boolean,
   187→): Promise<Response> {
   188→  return new Response(JSON.stringify({ authorized }), {
   189→    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   190→  })
   191→}
   192→
   193→// ── Recover purchase (sends magic link) ──────────────────────
   194→
   195→export async function handleRecoverPurchase(
   196→  request: Request,
   197→  env: Env,
   198→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   199→  sendEmail?: EmailSender,
   200→): Promise<Response> {
   201→  // Must be POST — state-changing operation
   202→  if (request.method !== 'POST') {
   203→    return new Response('Method not allowed', { status: 405, headers: NO_CACHE })
   204→  }
   205→
   206→  let body: { email?: string; book?: string }
   207→  try {
   208→    body = await request.json()
   209→  } catch {
   210→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid JSON body' }), {
   211→      status: 400,
   212→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   213→    })
   214→  }
   215→
   216→  const email = body.email?.trim().toLowerCase()
   217→  const bookSlug = body.book
   218→
   219→  if (!email || !bookSlug) {
   220→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Missing email or book' }), {
   221→      status: 400,
   222→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   223→    })
   224→  }
   225→
   226→  if (!EMAIL_RE.test(email)) {
   227→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid email format' }), {
   228→      status: 400,
   229→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   230→    })
   231→  }
   232→
   233→  if (!BOOK_SLUG_RE.test(bookSlug)) {
   234→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Invalid book slug' }), {
   235→      status: 400,
   236→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   237→    })
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   241→  if (!book) {
   242→    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Book not found' }), {
   243→      status: 400,
   244→      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   245→    })
   246→  }
   247→
   248→  // Look up purchase — but DON'T reveal whether the email exists.
   249→  // Always return the same success message to prevent email enumeration.
   250→  const record = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(`purchase:${email}:${bookSlug}`)
   251→
   252→  if (record) {
   253→    // Purchase exists — send magic link email
   254→    const baseUrl = getBaseUrl(request)
   255→    const token = await signMagicToken(
   256→      { email, bookSlug, exp: nowEpoch() + MAGIC_LINK_TTL },
   257→      env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   258→    )
   259→    const verifyUrl = `${baseUrl}/api/auth/verify?token=${encodeURIComponent(token)}`
   260→
   261→    const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === bookSlug)
   262→    const bookTitle = book?.paywall?.price
   263→      ? bookSlug // We don't have the book title in the manifest, use slug
   264→      : undefined
   265→
   266→    if (!sendEmail) {
   267→      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Email sending is not configured' }), {
   268→        status: 500,
   269→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   270→      })
   271→    }
   272→
   273→    try {
   274→      await sendEmail({
   275→        to: email,
   276→        subject: 'Recover your book purchase',
   277→        html: buildMagicLinkEmail(verifyUrl, bookTitle),
   278→        verifyUrl,
   279→        bookTitle,
   280→      })
   281→    } catch {
   282→      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: 'Failed to send email. Please try again.' }), {
   283→        status: 500,
   284→        headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE },
   285→      })
   286→    }
   287→  }
   288→
   289→  // Same response regardless of whether purchase was found (anti-enumeration)
   290→  return new Response(
   291→    JSON.stringify({ sent: true, message: 'If a purchase exists for this email, a recovery link has been sent.' }),
   292→    { headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...NO_CACHE } },
   293→  )
   294→}
   295→
   296→// ── Verify magic link token ──────────────────────────────────
   297→
   298→export async function handleVerifyToken(
   299→  request: Request,
   300→  env: Env,
   301→  manifest: { books: PressyBookManifest[] },
   302→): Promise<Response> {
   303→  const url = new URL(request.url)
   304→  const token = url.searchParams.get('token')
   305→
   306→  if (!token) {
   307→    return new Response('Missing token', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   308→  }
   309→
   310→  // Verify HMAC signature and expiration
   311→  const payload = await verifyMagicToken(token, env.COOKIE_SECRET)
   312→  if (!payload) {
   313→    return new Response('Invalid or expired link. Please request a new one.', {
   314→      status: 403,
   315→      headers: NO_CACHE,
   316→    })
   317→  }
   318→
   319→  // Validate payload fields
   320→  if (!EMAIL_RE.test(payload.email) || !BOOK_SLUG_RE.test(payload.bookSlug)) {
   321→    return new Response('Invalid token payload', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   322→  }
   323→
   324→  if (!manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === payload.bookSlug)) {
   325→    return new Response('Book not found', { status: 400, headers: NO_CACHE })
   326→  }
   327→
   328→  // Single-use: prevent replay (hash full token to avoid prefix collisions)
   329→  const tokenHash = await sha256hex(token)
   330→  const consumedKey = `magic-consumed:${tokenHash}`
   331→  const alreadyConsumed = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(consumedKey)
   332→  if (alreadyConsumed) {
   333→    return new Response('This link has already been used.', { status: 410, headers: NO_CACHE })
   334→  }
   335→
   336→  // Confirm the purchase still exists in KV
   337→  const record = await env.PRESSY_KV.get(`purchase:${payload.email}:${payload.bookSlug}`)
   338→  if (!record) {
   339→    return new Response('No purchase found', { status: 404, headers: NO_CACHE })
   340→  }
   341→
   342→  const purchase: PurchaseRecord = JSON.parse(record)
   343→  const book = manifest.books.find((b) => b.slug === payload.bookSlug)
   344→
   345→  // Mark token as consumed (keep for the token's TTL — 15 min is plenty)
   346→  await env.PRESSY_KV.put(consumedKey, '1', { expirationTtl: MAGIC_LINK_TTL })
   347→
   348→  // Issue auth cookie
   349→  const cookieValue = await signCookie(
   350→    {
   351→      book: payload.bookSlug,
   352→      email: payload.email,
   353→      purchasedAt: purchase.purchasedAt,
   354→      sessionId: purchase.sessionId,
   355→      exp: nowEpoch() + COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
   356→    },
   357→    env.COOKIE_SECRET,
   358→  )
   359→
   360→  // Redirect to the book home page so the user can click "Continue Reading"
   361→  const redirectPath = `/books/${payload.bookSlug}/`
   362→
   363→  return new Response(null, {
   364→    status: 302,
   365→    headers: {
   366→      Location: redirectPath,
   367→      'Set-Cookie': setCookieHeader(cookieName(payload.bookSlug), cookieValue),
   368→      ...NO_CACHE,
   369→    },
   370→  })
   371→}

1→/** 2→ * Build the magic link email HTML. 3→ */ 4→export function buildMagicLinkEmail(verifyUrl: string, bookTitle?: string): string { 5→ const title = bookTitle || 'your book' 6→ return ` 7→ 8→ 9→ 10→ 11→

Recover your purchase

12→

Click the link below to restore access to ${title}. This link expires in 15 minutes.

13→

14→ 15→ Restore Access 16→ 17→

18→

/** * Build the magic link email HTML. */ export function buildMagicLinkEmail(verifyUrl: string, bookTitle?: string): string { const title = bookTitle || 'your book' return `

Recover your purchase

Click the link below to restore access to ${title}. This link expires in 15 minutes.

Restore Access

If you didn't request this, you can safely ignore this email.

`.trim() }

1→/// <reference lib="webworker" />
     2→import { precacheAndRoute, cleanupOutdatedCaches } from 'workbox-precaching'
     3→import { registerRoute, NavigationRoute, Route } from 'workbox-routing'
     4→import { NetworkFirst, CacheFirst, StaleWhileRevalidate } from 'workbox-strategies'
     5→import { ExpirationPlugin } from 'workbox-expiration'
     6→import {
     7→  isBookChapterNavigation,
     8→  needsTrailingSlashRedirect,
     9→  normalizeCacheUrl,
    10→  prepareResponseForCache,
    11→} from './sw-logic'
    12→
    13→declare const self: ServiceWorkerGlobalScope
    14→
    15→const OFFLINE_CACHE = 'pressy-offline'
    16→// Use the SW's own location to derive the base path for offline.html,
    17→// so it works on subpath deployments (e.g. /pressy/pr-preview/pr-1/sw.js)
    18→const SW_BASE = new URL('./', (self as unknown as ServiceWorkerGlobalScope).location.href).pathname
    19→const OFFLINE_URL = `${SW_BASE}offline.html`
    20→const BOOK_CACHE = 'pressy-offline-books'
    21→
    22→// Precache all static assets (injected by build tool)
    23→precacheAndRoute(self.__WB_MANIFEST, {
    24→  ignoreURLParametersMatching: [/^page$/],
    25→})
    26→
    27→// Clean up old caches
    28→cleanupOutdatedCaches()
    29→
    30→// Cache the offline fallback page on install
    31→self.addEventListener('install', (event) => {
    32→  event.waitUntil(
    33→    caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE).then((cache) => cache.add(OFFLINE_URL))
    34→  )
    35→  self.skipWaiting()
    36→})
    37→
    38→self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {
    39→  event.waitUntil(self.clients.claim())
    40→})
    41→
    42→// Plugin that respects Cache-Control: no-store (workbox ignores it by default).
    43→// This prevents paywall/auth-gated responses from polluting the SW cache.
    44→const respectNoStore = {
    45→  cacheWillUpdate: async ({ response }: { response: Response }) => {
    46→    const cc = response.headers.get('Cache-Control')
    47→    if (cc && cc.includes('no-store')) return null
    48→    return response
    49→  },
    50→}
    51→
    52→// Navigation: NetworkFirst with offline fallback
    53→const navigationHandler = new NetworkFirst({
    54→  cacheName: 'pressy-pages',
    55→  networkTimeoutSeconds: 3,
    56→  plugins: [respectNoStore],
    57→})
    58→
    59→// Offline-cached book chapters: try the book cache first,
    60→// then fall through to the normal navigation handler.
    61→// Registered BEFORE the general NavigationRoute so book chapter
    62→// URLs are checked against the offline book cache first.
    63→// ignoreSearch: true so ?page=last (backward chapter nav) matches
    64→// the cached response for the base URL.
    65→registerRoute(
    66→  new Route(
    67→    ({ request, url }) => isBookChapterNavigation(url.pathname, request.mode),
    68→    async (params) => {
    69→      // Redirect non-trailing-slash URLs so relative asset paths in the
    70→      // HTML (e.g. ../../../assets/foo.js) resolve against the correct
    71→      // directory. Without the slash, the browser uses the parent path.
    72→      if (needsTrailingSlashRedirect(params.url.pathname)) {
    73→        const dest = new URL(params.url.href)
    74→        dest.pathname += '/'
    75→        return Response.redirect(dest.href, 302)
    76→      }
    77→
    78→      // Check offline book cache (trailing-slash keys)
    79→      const bookCache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
    80→      const cached = await bookCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
    81→      if (cached) return cached
    82→
    83→      // Fall through to network
    84→      try {
    85→        return await navigationHandler.handle(params)
    86→      } catch {
    87→        // Network failed — try the general navigation cache with ignoreSearch
    88→        // so ?page=last variants still hit cached chapter pages
    89→        const pagesCache = await caches.open('pressy-pages')
    90→        const pagesCached = await pagesCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
    91→        if (pagesCached) return pagesCached
    92→
    93→        const cache = await caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE)
    94→        const fallback = await cache.match(OFFLINE_URL)
    95→        return fallback || Response.error()
    96→      }
    97→    }
    98→  )
    99→)
   100→
   101→// General navigation: NetworkFirst with offline fallback
   102→registerRoute(
   103→  new NavigationRoute(async (params) => {
   104→    try {
   105→      return await navigationHandler.handle(params)
   106→    } catch {
   107→      // Network failed — try offline book cache and pressy-pages with
   108→      // ignoreSearch so ?page=last (backward chapter nav) still resolves
   109→      const bookCache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   110→      const bookCached = await bookCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
   111→      if (bookCached) return bookCached
   112→
   113→      const pagesCache = await caches.open('pressy-pages')
   114→      const pagesCached = await pagesCache.match(params.request, { ignoreSearch: true })
   115→      if (pagesCached) return pagesCached
   116→
   117→      // Last resort — serve offline fallback
   118→      const cache = await caches.open(OFFLINE_CACHE)
   119→      const fallback = await cache.match(OFFLINE_URL)
   120→      return fallback || Response.error()
   121→    }
   122→  })
   123→)
   124→
   125→// Cache images with CacheFirst strategy (exclude PWA icons — they're
   126→// updated on each deploy and should not be stuck behind a stale cache)
   127→registerRoute(
   128→  ({ request, url }) =>
   129→    request.destination === 'image' &&
   130→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/icon-192.png') &&
   131→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/icon-512.png') &&
   132→    !url.pathname.endsWith('/favicon.png'),
   133→  new CacheFirst({
   134→    cacheName: 'pressy-images',
   135→    plugins: [
   136→      new ExpirationPlugin({
   137→        maxEntries: 100,
   138→        maxAgeSeconds: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30, // 30 days
   139→      }),
   140→    ],
   141→  })
   142→)
   143→
   144→// Cache fonts with CacheFirst strategy
   145→registerRoute(
   146→  ({ request }) => request.destination === 'font',
   147→  new CacheFirst({
   148→    cacheName: 'pressy-fonts',
   149→    plugins: [
   150→      new ExpirationPlugin({
   151→        maxEntries: 20,
   152→        maxAgeSeconds: 60 * 60 * 24 * 365, // 1 year
   153→      }),
   154→    ],
   155→  })
   156→)
   157→
   158→// Cache CSS and JS with NetworkFirst so deploys are picked up immediately.
   159→// Vite hashes filenames so old URLs are never re-requested — the main
   160→// benefit of caching here is offline support, not speed.
   161→registerRoute(
   162→  ({ request }) =>
   163→    request.destination === 'style' || request.destination === 'script',
   164→  new NetworkFirst({
   165→    cacheName: 'pressy-static',
   166→    networkTimeoutSeconds: 3,
   167→    plugins: [respectNoStore],
   168→  })
   169→)
   170→
   171→// Message handler for manual cache operations
   172→self.addEventListener('message', async (event) => {
   173→  if (event.data?.type === 'CACHE_BOOK') {
   174→    const { bookSlug, urls } = event.data
   175→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   176→    let completed = 0
   177→
   178→    for (const url of urls) {
   179→      try {
   180→        const response = await fetch(url)
   181→        if (response.ok) {
   182→          // Strip the redirected flag — navigation requests reject
   183→          // already-followed redirect responses from the cache.
   184→          const clean = prepareResponseForCache(response)
   185→          // Normalize cache key to trailing-slash so it matches
   186→          // redirected navigation requests (e.g. /chapter-2/)
   187→          const cacheUrl = normalizeCacheUrl(url)
   188→          await cache.put(cacheUrl, clean)
   189→        }
   190→      } catch (err) {
   191→        console.error(`Failed to cache ${url}:`, err)
   192→      }
   193→
   194→      completed++
   195→      // Report progress back to client
   196→      event.source?.postMessage({
   197→        type: 'CACHE_PROGRESS',
   198→        bookSlug,
   199→        current: completed,
   200→        total: urls.length,
   201→      })
   202→    }
   203→
   204→    // Notify client that caching is complete
   205→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_COMPLETE', bookSlug, urls })
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  if (event.data?.type === 'GET_CACHE_STATUS') {
   209→    const { urls } = event.data
   210→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   211→    const cached: string[] = []
   212→
   213→    for (const url of urls) {
   214→      // Normalize to trailing-slash to match CACHE_BOOK keys
   215→      const matchUrl = normalizeCacheUrl(url)
   216→      const response = await cache.match(matchUrl)
   217→      if (response) {
   218→        cached.push(url)
   219→      }
   220→    }
   221→
   222→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_STATUS', cached })
   223→  }
   224→
   225→  if (event.data?.type === 'CLEAR_BOOK_CACHE') {
   226→    const { bookSlug } = event.data
   227→    const cache = await caches.open(BOOK_CACHE)
   228→    const keys = await cache.keys()
   229→
   230→    for (const request of keys) {
   231→      if (request.url.includes(`/books/${bookSlug}`)) {
   232→        await cache.delete(request)
   233→      }
   234→    }
   235→
   236→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'CACHE_CLEARED', bookSlug })
   237→  }
   238→
   239→  if (event.data?.type === 'INVALIDATE_BOOK_PAGES') {
   240→    const { bookSlug } = event.data
   241→    const cacheNames = ['pressy-pages', 'pressy-static']
   242→    for (const name of cacheNames) {
   243→      const cache = await caches.open(name)
   244→      const keys = await cache.keys()
   245→      for (const request of keys) {
   246→        if (request.url.includes(`/books/${bookSlug}`)) {
   247→          await cache.delete(request)
   248→        }
   249→      }
   250→    }
   251→    event.source?.postMessage({ type: 'BOOK_PAGES_INVALIDATED', bookSlug })
   252→  }
   253→
   254→  if (event.data?.type === 'SKIP_WAITING') {
   255→    self.skipWaiting()
   256→  }
   257→})

    27→// IndexedDB for reading progress
    28→const DB_NAME = 'pressy'
    29→const DB_VERSION = 2
    30→const PROGRESS_STORE = 'reading-progress'
    31→const UNLOCKS_STORE = 'unlocks'
    32→const BOOKMARKS_STORE = 'bookmarks'
    33→
    34→let db: IDBDatabase | null = null
    35→
    36→async function initDB(): Promise<IDBDatabase> {
    37→  if (db) return db
    38→
    39→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    40→    const request = indexedDB.open(DB_NAME, DB_VERSION)
    41→
    42→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    43→    request.onblocked = () => reject(new Error('Database upgrade blocked by open connection'))
    44→    request.onsuccess = () => {
    45→      db = request.result
    46→      resolve(db)
    47→    }
    48→
    49→    request.onupgradeneeded = (event) => {
    50→      const database = (event.target as IDBOpenDBRequest).result
    51→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(PROGRESS_STORE)) {
    52→        database.createObjectStore(PROGRESS_STORE, { keyPath: 'chapterSlug' })
    53→      }
    54→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(UNLOCKS_STORE)) {
    55→        database.createObjectStore(UNLOCKS_STORE, { keyPath: 'bookSlug' })
    56→      }
    57→      if (!database.objectStoreNames.contains(BOOKMARKS_STORE)) {
    58→        database.createObjectStore(BOOKMARKS_STORE, { keyPath: 'id' })
    59→      }
    60→    }
    61→  })
    62→}
    63→
    64→export async function saveReadingProgress(progress: ReadingProgress): Promise<void> {
    65→  const database = await initDB()
    66→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    67→    const tx = database.transaction(PROGRESS_STORE, 'readwrite')
    68→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)
    69→    const request = store.put(progress)
    70→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve()
    71→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    72→  })
    73→}
    74→
    75→export async function getReadingProgress(chapterSlug: string): Promise<ReadingProgress | null> {
    76→  const database = await initDB()
    77→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    78→    const tx = database.transaction(PROGRESS_STORE, 'readonly')
    79→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)
    80→    const request = store.get(chapterSlug)
    81→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve(request.result || null)
    82→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    83→  })
    84→}
    85→
    86→export async function getAllReadingProgress(): Promise<ReadingProgress[]> {
    87→  const database = await initDB()
    88→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    89→    const tx = database.transaction(PROGRESS_STORE, 'readonly')
    90→    const store = tx.objectStore(PROGRESS_STORE)
    91→    const request = store.getAll()
    92→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve(request.result || [])
    93→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
    94→  })
    95→}
    96→
    97→export async function isBookUnlocked(bookSlug: string): Promise<boolean> {
    98→  const database = await initDB()
    99→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   100→    const tx = database.transaction(UNLOCKS_STORE, 'readonly')
   101→    const store = tx.objectStore(UNLOCKS_STORE)
   102→    const request = store.get(bookSlug)
   103→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve(!!request.result)
   104→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
   105→  })
   106→}
   107→
   108→export async function unlockBook(bookSlug: string, orderId?: string): Promise<void> {
   109→  const database = await initDB()
   110→  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   111→    const tx = database.transaction(UNLOCKS_STORE, 'readwrite')
   112→    const store = tx.objectStore(UNLOCKS_STORE)
   113→    const request = store.put({ bookSlug, orderId, unlockedAt: Date.now() })
   114→    request.onsuccess = () => resolve()
   115→    request.onerror = () => reject(request.error)
   116→  })
   117→}

  1131→export function hydrate(data: HydrateData, Content?: ComponentType, chapterMapData?: ChapterMapData): void {
  1132→  basePath = getBasePath(data.route)
  1133→  currentRoute.value = data.route
  1134→
  1135→  // Auto-redirect to last-read chapter on fresh app/tab open.
  1136→  // Clear the session flag when the app goes to background so the next
  1137→  // foreground open is treated as fresh (Android PWAs don't reliably
  1138→  // clear sessionStorage when swiped away).
  1139→  const isNewSession = !sessionStorage.getItem('pressy-session-active')
  1140→  sessionStorage.setItem('pressy-session-active', '1')
  1141→  document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => {
  1142→    if (document.visibilityState === 'hidden') {
  1143→      sessionStorage.removeItem('pressy-session-active')
  1144→    }
  1145→  })
  1146→
  1147→  // Skip auto-redirect if this navigation came from within the reader
  1148→  // (e.g. backward from first chapter → book TOC). The flag is set by the
  1149→  // Reader component before window.location.href assignments.
  1150→  const isInternalNav = sessionStorage.getItem('pressy-internal-nav')
  1151→  sessionStorage.removeItem('pressy-internal-nav')
  1152→
  1153→  if (isNewSession && !isInternalNav && (data.routeType === 'home' || data.routeType === 'book')) {
  1154→    const lastRead = localStorage.getItem('pressy-last-read')
  1155→    if (lastRead) {
  1156→      try {
  1157→        const { bookSlug, chapterSlug } = JSON.parse(lastRead)
  1158→        const book = data.manifest.books.find(b => b.slug === bookSlug)
  1159→        if (book?.chapters.some(ch => ch.slug === chapterSlug)) {
  1160→          window.location.replace(`${basePath}/books/${bookSlug}/${chapterSlug}`)
  1161→          return
  1162→        }
  1163→      } catch {
  1164→        // Invalid JSON in localStorage, ignore
  1165→      }
  1166→    }
  1167→  }
  1168→
  1169→  // Initialize
  1170→  loadTheme()
  1171→  setupOfflineDetection()
  1172→  setupInstallPrompt()
  1173→  initDB()
  1174→
  1175→  // Register service worker for PWA offline support
  1176→  registerServiceWorker(basePath)
  1177→
  1178→  // Auto-download books for offline when the user installs the PWA
  1179→  window.addEventListener('appinstalled', () => {
  1180→    for (const book of data.manifest.books) {
  1181→      if (isBookCached(book.slug)) continue
  1182→      const chapterUrls = book.chapters.map(ch => `${basePath}/books/${book.slug}/${ch.slug}`)
  1183→      downloadBookForOffline(book.slug, chapterUrls)
  1184→    }

content: Okay, given this revised report, which should we actually fix. In my opinion, only high severity ones, or ones that have a very simple and straightforward fix

content.text:
Now I have all the information. Here's the verified audit:

---

# Verified Security Audit

I read every relevant source file. Here's each finding, whether it's real, and its actual severity.

## CRITICAL — Confirmed

### 1. Paid content accessible as static JS chunks without auth
**Real. Critical.** `plugin.ts:467-468` compiles every chapter (including paid) into separate JS chunks via `import()`. The Cloudflare middleware at `middleware.ts:31-35` only guards `/books/{slug}/{slug}` HTML pages. The `/assets/*.js` chunks containing the actual chapter text pass through unguarded at line 134 (`return next()`). Confirmed in the flatland build output — every chapter is a separate `.js` file in `dist/assets/`. This is the fundamental architectural issue.

### 2. Paywall fails open on network error
**Real. High (not Critical).** `client.tsx:591`: `.catch(() => setPaywallAuthorized(true))`. However, this only matters if the Cloudflare middleware is deployed, because it controls whether the HTML page is served at all. If the middleware is present, this client-side check is secondary defense — the server already served the real page. If the middleware is absent, this combined with #1 means content is accessible. Downgrading to **High** because it's a secondary defense layer.

### 3. Full manifest with absolute build paths embedded in every page
**Real. Medium (not Critical).** Confirmed in built output — `dist/assets/books-DJjEpBhf.js` contains strings like `/Users/blittle/dev/pressy/examples/flatland/content/books/flatland/00-preface.mdx`. Leaks username and filesystem layout. The `stripePriceId` is also exposed but is a read-only identifier. This is information disclosure, not a direct exploitation path. **Medium.**

### 4. `RESEND_API_KEY` logged to console
**Real. High.** `examples/moby-dick/functions/_middleware.ts:8`: `console.log("RESEND_API_KEY:", env.RESEND_API_KEY)` — logs the API key on every email send to Cloudflare Workers logs.

## HIGH — Verified

### 5. `JSON.stringify` in `<script>` tag without `</script>` escaping
**Real. High.** `plugin.ts:274`: `const data = ${dataJson};` inside a `<script>` tag. `JSON.stringify` does not escape `</script>`. If any manifest field (book title, description from YAML frontmatter) contains `</script>`, it breaks out of the script context. The author controls this data, so it requires a malicious or careless author, but it's a real XSS sink.

**However** — in the production build (`plugin.ts:712`), the HTML uses `<script type="module" src="...">` with an external file, NOT inline JSON. The vulnerable inline version is **only used in dev mode** (the `generateHTML` function at line 214 is for Vite's dev server transform). This lowers severity somewhat but dev-mode XSS is still real. **Medium.**

### 6. `document.write()` with fetched HTML in paywall page
**Real, but Low (not High).** `middleware.ts:318-321`. The `document.write(html)` only runs after the server confirms authorization at line 299 (`if (data.authorized)`). The HTML is fetched from the same origin with cookies. For this to be exploitable, an attacker would need to compromise the server response — at which point they already have full control. The fetch is same-origin with `credentials: "include"`, not cross-origin. **Low** — the real risk is in other XSS vectors, not this one.

### 7. Unescaped `siteTitle` in offline page HTML
**Real, but Low (not High).** `offline-page.ts:8`: `<title>Offline | ${siteTitle}</title>`. The `siteTitle` comes from `config.site.title` in `pressy.config.ts`, which is authored by the site owner, not user input. Self-XSS from your own config file. **Low.**

### 8. No Content Security Policy
**Real. Medium (not High).** No CSP anywhere — confirmed. This is a defense-in-depth gap, not a vulnerability by itself. It amplifies any XSS that does exist. **Medium.**

### 9. SW cache poisoning via `CACHE_BOOK` message
**Overstated. Low (not High).** `sw.ts:172-206`. The `CACHE_BOOK` handler fetches URLs and caches responses. But: (a) `postMessage` to a service worker can only come from pages within the SW's scope (same origin), (b) the SW fetches the URL via same-origin `fetch()`, and (c) `X-Frame-Options: DENY` prevents framing. An attacker would need existing XSS on the origin to exploit this, at which point they already have full control. **Low.**

### 10. Rate limiter TOCTOU race condition
**Real. Medium (not High).** `middleware.ts:69-83`. The non-atomic `get`/`put` on KV is real. Concurrent requests bypass the limit. But: (a) this protects auth endpoints, not the paywall itself, (b) the practical impact is allowing more brute-force attempts, and (c) the signed cookies/tokens can't be feasibly brute-forced regardless. **Medium.**

### 11. Paid content cached offline after authorized download
**Real, but expected behavior. Low (not High).** `sw.ts:178-189`. When an authorized user downloads for offline, the content is cached. This is the intended feature — it's like saving a purchased ebook. The content was already authorized when cached. Cookie expiry doesn't mean the purchase was revoked. **Low** — this is a design decision, not a bug.

### 12. Email sending failure breaks anti-enumeration
**Real. Medium.** `stripe.ts:266-286`. If `sendEmail` is undefined (line 266) or throws (line 281), the function returns 500 — but only inside the `if (record)` block. Non-existent emails reach line 290 and return 200. So 500 = purchase exists, 200 = doesn't. **Medium.**

### 13. GitHub Actions not SHA-pinned
**Real. Low (not High).** Third-party actions `peaceiris/actions-gh-pages@v4` and `rossjrw/pr-preview-action@v1` use mutable tags. This is common practice and the actions are widely used. Best practice is to pin, but the practical risk is low for a small project. **Low.**

## MEDIUM — Verified

### 14. Magic link TOCTOU race
**Real. Low (not Medium).** `stripe.ts:328-346`. Same `get`/`put` pattern. Two concurrent requests with the same token could both issue cookies. But: both cookies are for the same user/book with the same data, so the impact is just "two valid cookies for the same purchase." **Low.**

### 15. No CSRF on `/api/auth/recover`
**Real. Low (not Medium).** The endpoint sends recovery emails. A cross-origin attacker could trigger emails to known addresses. But: (a) `SameSite=Lax` prevents cookie attachment, (b) the endpoint doesn't use cookies for auth — it takes email from the body, (c) the rate limit (3/15min) caps abuse, (d) the impact is nuisance emails only. **Low.**

### 16. Email enumeration via timing side-channel
**Real, but Low (not Medium).** The found-purchase path does more work (sign token, call email API). Measurable in theory. In practice, the email API latency variance dwarfs the HMAC computation time, making statistical discrimination unreliable. **Low.**

### 17. Unescaped `pwaConfig.shortName` in HTML meta
**Real, but Low (not Medium).** `plugin.ts:252`: `content="${pwaConfig.shortName}"`. This comes from the author's `pressy.config.ts`. Self-XSS from your own config. **Low.**

### 18. `escapeHtmlAttr` missing single-quote escaping
**Real. Info (not Medium).** `plugin.ts:210-212`. All templates use double-quoted attributes. No current path uses single-quote delimiters. Incomplete as a general utility, but not currently exploitable. **Info.**

### 19. Unescaped `price` in paywall page HTML
**Real. Low (not Medium).** `middleware.ts:277`: `Purchase${price ? ' — ' + price : ''}`. The `price` comes from the manifest's `book.paywall.price`, which is set by the author in `_book.yaml`. Self-XSS from your own metadata. **Low.**

### 20. IP rate limiting falls back to spoofable `x-forwarded-for`
**Real, but Info (not Medium).** `middleware.ts:85-89`. On Cloudflare Workers, `cf-connecting-ip` is always present — Cloudflare sets it. The `x-forwarded-for` fallback only applies outside Cloudflare (local dev). In production this is a non-issue. **Info.**

### 21. Rate limit window resets on every request
**Real. Low (not Medium).** `middleware.ts:81`. The `expirationTtl` resets on each `put`. This makes the window sliding rather than fixed. It's actually *more restrictive* for legitimate users, not less. The TOCTOU race (#10) is the real problem. **Low.**

### 22. Overly broad CI permissions
**Real. Low (not Medium).** `deploy-pages.yml:11-13`. `contents: write` + `pull-requests: write` at top level. The `pull_request` trigger (not `pull_request_target`) limits what fork PRs can do — GitHub restricts the token automatically. **Low.**

### 23. `.gitignore` missing `.env*` patterns
**Real. Low (not Medium).** No `.env*` pattern. But no `.env` files exist or were ever committed. This is a "safety net" gap, not an active vulnerability. **Low.**

### 24. `postMessage` handlers lack origin validation
**Not a real issue. Drop.** Service worker `message` events can only come from controlled clients (same-origin pages). The SW API enforces this at the browser level. Origin validation is not needed.

### 25. No SRI on resources
**Not a real issue. Drop.** The resources are self-hosted, not loaded from CDNs. SRI protects against CDN compromise; for self-hosted assets, if the server is compromised, the attacker can change the integrity hashes too. In production builds, Vite uses content-hashed filenames.

## LOW — Verified

| # | Finding | Verdict |
|---|---------|---------|
| 26 | HKDF zero salt | **Real. Info.** Context labels provide separation. RFC 5869 allows zero salt. |
| 27 | Cookie `Path=/` | **Not an issue. Drop.** The payload contains the `book` field which is checked. |
| 28 | 1-year cookie, no revocation | **Real. Low.** Deliberate design tradeoff for stateless tokens. |
| 29 | Manifest cached in module variable | **Real. Low.** Worker isolate recycling handles this. |
| 30 | `session_id` used as KV key without validation | **Not an issue. Drop.** Stripe rejects invalid IDs. KV keys with arbitrary strings cause no harm. |
| 31 | Cookie name from unvalidated slug | **Not an issue. Drop.** `handleAuthCallback` gets the slug from Stripe session metadata — Stripe-controlled data. |
| 32 | Email in localStorage | **Real. Info.** Only in the unused `Paywall.tsx` component. |
| 33 | localStorage-driven navigation | **Not an issue. Drop.** The slugs are validated against the manifest (`book?.chapters.some(ch => ch.slug === chapterSlug)`) at `client.tsx:1159`. No open redirect. |
| 34 | Unescaped values in email HTML | **Real. Low.** `bookTitle` is the slug (author-controlled). `verifyUrl` uses `encodeURIComponent` for the token. |
| 35 | Unescaped `config.site.language` | **Real. Info.** Author's config. Typical values: "en", "fr". |
| 36 | KV namespace ID in wrangler.toml | **Not an issue. Drop.** KV namespace IDs are not secrets. |

## Unused Code: `Paywall.tsx` component
The `<Paywall>` component is **exported but never rendered** anywhere — `<Paywall` appears in zero JSX files. It's dead code with a localStorage-based "bypass" that doesn't matter because it's never used. The real paywall enforcement is the Cloudflare middleware + `client.tsx`'s `usePaywallAuthorized` hook. All findings about `Paywall.tsx` (localStorage bypass, email storage) are **moot**.

Similarly, `isBookUnlocked` and `unlockBook` in `client.tsx` are exported but never called anywhere. Dead code.

---

## Revised Summary

| Severity | Count | Key Findings |
|----------|-------|-------------|
| **Critical** | 1 | #1: Paid chapter JS chunks served without auth |
| **High** | 2 | #2: Paywall fails open on network error, #4: API key logged |
| **Medium** | 5 | #3: Build paths leaked, #5: `</script>` in dev mode, #8: No CSP, #10: Rate limit TOCTOU, #12: Anti-enumeration broken on error |
| **Low** | 10 | #7, #11, #13, #14, #15, #16, #17, #19, #21-23 |
| **Info** | 4 | #18, #20, #26, #35 |
| **Dropped** | 9 | Non-issues or dead code: #9, #24, #25, #27, #30, #31, #32, #33, #36 |

The original audit had 36 findings. After verification: **22 are real issues** (1 critical, 2 high, 5 medium), **5 are info-level**, and **9 should be dropped** as non-issues or dead code.

message.content: Okay, given this revised report, which should we actually fix. In my opinion, only high severity ones, or ones that have a very simple and straightforward fix

content.text:
Agreed. Here's what I'd fix:

**Must fix:**
1. **#4: API key logged** — Delete the `console.log` line. One-line fix.
2. **#2: Paywall fails open** — Change `true` to `false` in the `.catch()`. One-line fix.

**Easy enough to fix alongside:**
3. **#12: Anti-enumeration broken on error** — Move the `sendEmail` check and try/catch outside the `if (record)` block (or return the same 200 for all paths). Small refactor.
4. **#5: `</script>` in dev-mode JSON** — Add `.replace(/</g, '\\u003c')` after `JSON.stringify`. One-liner.
5. **#3: Build paths leaked** — Strip `filePath`, `basePath`, `coverPath` from the manifest before serializing to the client. Small change in `plugin.ts`.

**Skip for now:**
- **#1 (static JS chunks)** — Architectural. Fixing this requires server-side rendering or chunk encryption. Not a quick fix.
- **#8 (CSP), #10 (rate limiter TOCTOU)** — Real but not simple. CSP requires auditing all inline scripts. Rate limiting needs Durable Objects or a different backend.
- Everything else is Low/Info and not worth the churn.

Want me to go ahead and fix those 5?

message.content: yes, then create a PR afterwards

file.content:
export interface BookMetadata {
  title: string
  author: string
  description?: string
  cover?: string
  publishDate?: string
  isbn?: string
  language?: string
  paywall?: {
    enabled: boolean
    previewChapters: number
    price?: string
    stripePriceId?: string
    mode?: 'email' | 'stripe'
  }
}

export interface ArticleMetadata {
  title: string
  author: string
  description?: string
  publishDate?: string
  tags?: string[]
  cover?: string
}

export interface Chapter {
  slug: string
  title: string
  order: number
  filePath: string
  content?: string
  wordCount?: number
  readingTime?: number
}

export interface Book {
  slug: string
  metadata: BookMetadata
  chapters: Chapter[]
  basePath: string
  coverPath?: string
  coverUrl?: string
}

export interface Article {
  slug: string
  metadata: ArticleMetadata
  filePath: string
  basePath: string
  content?: string
  wordCount?: number
  readingTime?: number
}

export type ContentItem = Book | Article

export interface ContentManifest {
  books: Book[]
  articles: Article[]
}

export interface Route {
  path: string
  type: 'book' | 'article' | 'chapter' | 'home' | 'books' | 'articles'
  content?: ContentItem | Chapter
  book?: Book
}

export interface ReadingProgress {
  chapterSlug: string
  page: number
  totalPages: number
  scrollPosition: number
  timestamp: number
}

export interface Bookmark {
  id: string              // "bookSlug:chapterSlug:timestamp"
  bookSlug: string
  chapterSlug: string
  chapterTitle: string
  page: number
  totalPages: number
  scrollPosition: number
  createdAt: number
}

export interface ChapterMapData {
  chapterMap: Record<string, () => Promise<{ default: import('preact').ComponentType<{ components?: Record<string, unknown> }> }>>
  chapterOrder: string[]
}

git add examples/moby-dick/functions/_middleware.ts packages/@pressy/cloudflare/src/stripe.ts packages/pressy/src/runtime/client.tsx packages/pressy/src/vite/plugin.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Fix 5 security issues found during audit

- Remove console.log of RESEND_API_KEY in moby-dick middleware
- Change paywall auth check to fail closed on network error
- Fix anti-enumeration leak: return same response on email send failure
- Escape JSON in inline <script> tags to prevent </script> breakout
- Strip build-time paths (filePath, basePath, coverPath) from client manifest

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
